records.description: Wafer builds AI agents that work as autonomous performance engineers, optimizing GPU kernels for AI inference. Our customers are chip companies and cloud providers who need their AI models running at peak performance on any type of hardware. Our founding team includes engineers from Google (Spanner, Gemini), Two Sigma, AWS, and Argonne National Lab, with NeurIPS publications in ML.
records.launchTitle: Wafer Pass: flat-rate access to the fastest open-source LLMs
records.tagline: The fastest open-source LLMs for OpenClaw, Claude Code, and any agent harness
records.tags: AI
records.launchBodyText: TL;DR Wafer Pass is a single subscription that gives you the fastest optimized open source LLMs for any agentic coding harness. Plans start at $10/week. Every plan includes every model we host. Sign up at wafer.ai/pass (https://www.wafer.ai/ pricing). Hi YC! We're Emilio and Steven, founders of Wafer (S25) Today we're launching Wafer Pass , a flat rate API subscription to the fastest optimized open source LLMs. One key, every model, drops into Claude Code, OpenClaw, Cline, Kilo Code, Roo Code, OpenHands, or Conductor. The problem If you use AI coding agents, you've felt the pain. Usage caps that hit at the worst moment. Per token bills that swing between $5 and $50 a session. Juggling API keys across providers. Watching frontier closed models throttle you on Friday afternoons. Open source models (Qwen3.5 397B, GLM5.1, DeepSeek V4 Pro) have closed most of the gap on coding tasks. The problem is getting them served at the speed you actually want for an agent loop. Most providers run them on stock SGLang or vLLM, which leaves 50–80% of GPU performance on the table. What we built Wafer's core product is an AI performance engineer. You point it at an LLM and it optimizes it. We have been pointing Wafer at the leading open source LLMs and let it rewrite the entire serving stack: kernels, batching, scheduling, memory layout. The results are really impressive so far: Qwen3.5 397B Turbo is 2.8x faster than base SGLang GLM5.1 Turbo is 2x faster than vLLM baseline DeepSeek V4 Pro is 2x faster than vLLM baseline The Qwen example: Same weights as anywhere else. Just faster, because Wafer rewrote the kernels. We put the result behind a single OpenAI and Anthropic compatible API. Wafer Pass One subscription. One API key. Every model we host. $10/week (Starter): 1,000 requests every 5 hours. All models. $25/week (Privacy): 2,000 requests every 5 hours. All models. Zero Data Retention. Every plan includes every model, and every future Turbo model we ship. No price increase as the catalog grows. For context: that's 10x requests than Claude Code Max, at 1/2 the price. Try it Sign up: wafer.ai/pass (https://wafer.ai/pass) If you use Claude Code, OpenClaw, or any other agent harness, we'd love your feedback. Drop your Wafer API key in and tell us what breaks. If you're building an agent or coding tool and want a default model layer, let's talk. Email: emilio@wafer.ai (mailto:emilio@wafer.ai) · Book a 15 min call: cal.com/wafer/quick chat

records.launchTitle: HEVN: Brex for Non-US Internet Companies
records.tagline: Operate in USD — without needing a US entity.
records.tags: Finance, Consumer Finance, Neobank
records.launchBodyText: HEVN: Brex for non US companies \ \ Tl;Dr;\ \ \ Banking for non US internet companies is hard. HEVN provides an alternative: open virtual bank accounts in 1 day, get USD cards with 0% FX, and send/receive ACH, SEPA, wires, and USDC. The problem For non US companies, USD banking takes months, requires travel, and often breaks after the first transactions — making it hard to get paid via Stripe, Apple, or run global payroll. In practice: 2 20 weeks to open an account in UAE or Hong Kong Founders have to fly in person just to pass KYC $20K+ per year in legal and banking fees Accounts get frozen after first SWIFT transfer Visa charge 2 3% FX fee on USD payments At the same time, Brex, Ramp, and Mercury only support US companies with an EIN. What HEVN does HEVN is a B2B financial platform that lets global internet companies operate in USD — without needing a US entity. Run your business in USD with corporate cards and global payments with 0% FX fees Get named local accounts in EU, US, HK and UAE banks in 1 day Send and receive ACH / SEPA / USDC / wires worldwide (soon) Store USD in FDIC insured US accounts, even without an EIN Video Launch \ <https://youtu.be/RBr87D4AZKk Backstory We previously built HOT Labs, a Web3 infrastructure company behind a 15M user wallet.\ \ While operating globally, we kept running into the same problem: banking didn’t work. One of us spent 6 months opening a UAE account — only to face an AML check right after the first transaction Another had to fly twice just to open an account We paid \ $24K/year through a law firm just to maintain banking access — without card support We worked with dozens companies, and most of them ended up using stablecoins because traditional banking failed. Now 12 of them our customers. We built HEVN to help global internet companies focus on running and scaling their business, with the same access to US financial services as US companies. Today, we’re fully ready to serve cumpanies in the UAE and are looking to onboard pilot customers in Hong Kong and Singapore. Asks Do you know any non US internet company? maybe a design studio in Serbia, marketing agency in Hong Kong, or a law firm in the UAE. All of them can start receiving local payments via ACH and run global payrolls. Did you work at Stripe or Apple Store before? Any introductions to the team that could help us set up Stripe/Apple payouts to virtual bank accounts would be very helpful. Are you a YC company based internationally? you can use HEVN for global baking or stablecoins completely free Book a demo or contact: pv@hevn.finance (mailto:pv@hevn.finance) \ \ HEVN is a financial technology company, not a bank. Banking services are provided by regulated partner institutions.

records.tags: Artificial Intelligence, B2B, Productivity, Sales, Sales Enablement
records.launchBodyText: Hey YC 👋 We're Max, Carina and Alan, founders of Warmly.ai. TL;DR : We just launched AI Autopilot : an always on agent that lives on every page of your website, identifies visitors at the person level before any form fill, gives demos, qualifies prospects, books meetings, and retargets the rest. Customers running it are converting 30% more inbound traffic. Setup is a script tag and 30 minutes. Launch announcement here (https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7455240801065885696/) (would greatly appreciate a comment!). <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EHybZmEzyTs The Problem Most B2B SaaS companies are bleeding pipeline through their front door. Your website is your highest leverage asset. Every visitor you've paid for lands there. They often have the problem you solve but you just might not be fast enough to get to them in time before they leave or go to a competitor. Buyers now expect to talk to someone immediately. They don’t want to wait a day to schedule a call with an SDR to get a basic question answered. But every "AI chatbot" we've seen since 2023 has the same architecture under the hood: a stateless GPT wrapper that rebuilds context from scratch on every message, hallucinates, and hands off "qualified" leads with no traceability. AEs aren’t fans and buyers see through them in two messages. What we built <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=KkvDdJCsqd8 AI Autopilot is one agent on every page of your site. It pulls from your website, help center, internal docs, sales calls, sales methodologies, and CRM. It picks its own qualification path. It generates slide decks on the fly mid conversation, triggers the right videos by intent, and offers an optional video avatar so visitors talk to a person instead of a widget. When the visitor doesn't book? AI Autopilot doesn't give up. It Slacks your team, sends a personalized follow up email, and triggers LinkedIn retargeting for your top prospects. It self improves. It analyzes every conversation against the goals you set, tunes itself, and is version controlled. You can watch it get better over time. In our beta accounts, automated inbound meetings increased 30%. Why now We started out compiling signals for B2B sales/marketing teams, like person level website visitor data. In the past year we took a different bet: build a context graph for B2B GTM first, then put agents on top of it. The context graph is a unified, real time data layer that connects every signal, action, note, and outcome for every account. When AI Autopilot engages a visitor, it knows they saw your LinkedIn ad last week, your SDR sent a follow up email yesterday, and they downloaded a case study an hour ago. It doesn't have to "look this up.” The context is already pre computed in primitive stores it can query in milliseconds. A blog post we wrote that covers how we think about the GTM space: GTM Brain: Next Platform Owns Decisions, Not Data (https://www.warmly.ai/blog/gtm brain). Asks for the YC community 1\. Drop us a comment on our launch post (https://www.linkedin.com/feed/update/urn:li:activity:7455240801065885696/) . We’d love for more of our community to know us for building GTM agents and not just supply GTM data :). 2\. Try us out. Book a demo with us if this is something you’re interested in! <https://www.warmly.ai/p/book a demo Warmly, Max, Carina & Alan

records.launchBodyText: The problem Factoring and ABL is a $4T+ industry running on 1990s software . There is no automation layer. Every new client means more headcount, not better software. In practice: For a $500M AUM lender, teams spend 6+ hrs/day matching messy wire payments, with 16–20 errors/month and 3 day processing cycles. 25 people verify invoices at $2–5 each, and funding is delayed 1–3 days. Account managers chase 50–100 borrowers/day, with payment proof lost in emails, spreadsheets, and SMS threads. What Zolvo does Zolvo is giving lenders the infrastructure they need to scale efficiently while maintaining total visibility and control over their risk . We sit as the AI layer between bank accounts, loan management systems, and ops teams. Our platform syncs directly with bank accounts, communication channels, and LMS/FMS to handle reconciliation and collections automatically. By turning these manual chores into background processes, your team now reviews only the exceptions. Video Launch <https://youtu.be/Mku41 sYeXk Traction Our first client paid an annual contract before we had a product and became an angel investor two weeks in. We went live in less than a month and replaced work that would have required a team of 14 people. Asks 1. Do you know anyone in factoring or ABL? One warm intro is worth more than any outreach we run. 2. We’re looking to partner with private credit funds. We’re building the monitoring and reporting layer their portfolio companies need, and they’re great distribution partners. Book a demo or contact: isa@zolvo.com (mailto:isa@zolvo.com) Why us Our first paying client signed during the YC application process, before a single line of code was written. I was the first hire at Domu (YC S24), where we went from $0 to $1M ARR. Tony was a founding AI engineer at Domu too, running 100K+ AI calls/day with zero churn.

records.tagline: AI broker connecting American brands with the world's best factories.
records.tags: Artificial Intelligence, B2B, Supply Chain
records.launchBodyText: Hey 👋 We're Edward and Jennifer, founders of Saudara AI . TL;DR: We're an AI broker connecting American brands with the world’s best international factories. Saudara is built by a third generation manufacturer and an Amazon supply chain veteran, two people who've spent their careers on both sides of this problem. We don’t just sell software, we sell outcomes and services. Real humans do the work, supercharged by AI, at 10x the speed and a fraction of the cost of traditional sourcing agents. International sourcing is hard. We make it easy. <https://youtu.be/tna8cqJsfok ❌ The Problem Sourcing overseas should be straightforward. It never is. Five traps catch almost every foreign buyer, and most never see it coming. Trust — Suppliers overpromise, cut corners on mass production, and go quiet after the deposit clears. Price — Unbeatable quotes hide lower grade materials, hidden fees, and cost hikes once you're locked in. Timing — Missed deadlines kill holiday season sales, trigger chargebacks, and derail launches. Payment terms — New buyers pay 100% upfront. Established buyers get net 60. The playing field has never been level. Scalability — A supplier that works at 500 units may fall apart at 50,000. ✅ Our Solution Saudara means "family" in Indonesian. Jennifer's family has spent 50+ years building relationships across Asian manufacturing. We don't blindly search for suppliers. We have their personal numbers. We're a broker, not a marketplace. You tell us what you need, we deliver it. Our relationships get us into factories that don't take cold inquiries. Our AI handles the rest, vetting, benchmarking, negotiation support, QC tracking, and logistics, end to end. Trust, protected — Our AI flags supplier inconsistencies and tells you when something looks too good to be true. Price transparency — Every quote benchmarked against real market data. You always know what's fair. Specs, timelines, and QC — Spec sheets locked before approval, milestones tracked proactively, quality checked from raw materials to shipment. Better payment terms — We negotiate 30/70 and net 60 on your behalf. The terms established buyers take for granted, available from day one. Suppliers that can scale — Matched to your volume today and where you're going tomorrow. 🌿 Starting With Indonesia? We're starting with Indonesia because it's where we're from, and because most of it is still inaccessible to Western buyers without the right relationships. We have them. Palm oil, vanilla, nickel, rattan, patchouli, charcoal briquettes, handcrafted furniture from Jepara, and one of Southeast Asia's strongest apparel manufacturing bases. Resource rich, largely untapped, and relationship gated. 👫 The Team Edward Haryono — Grew up around Indonesian logistics. Amazon supply chain background. President of IABA PNW (Non profit promoting Indonesia and Pacific Northwest Economic Relations) Jennifer Prasetyo — Third generation Indonesian manufacturer. Nationally trusted sourcing expert behind @jenandtech (https://www.instagram.com/jenandtech/) (95K+ followers across all socials). Source Smarter Stop gambling on suppliers. If you or someone you know is sourcing products internationally, send them our way. Start with Saudara. (https://saudara.ai) Or, book a call here (https://calendly.com/saudara/start sourcing)

records.description: Miniloop is a workflow engine for building reliable AI systems. It turns one-off prompts into structured, reusable workflows with enforced context, typed outputs, and safe re-runs, making AI dependable in production environments.
records.launchTitle: Miniloop — AI GTM teammates for startups
records.tagline: Run outbound, follow-up, content, and ops without hiring.
records.tags: AI, AI Assistant
records.launchBodyText: Hey everyone 👋 We’re building Miniloop: an AI workforce platform that helps startups run GTM without hiring a full GTM team. TL;DR Miniloop lets startups hire AI GTM teammates that run outbound, follow up with leads, create content, qualify signups, update CRM/spreadsheets, and execute repetitive workflows across tools like Gmail, Apollo, HubSpot, Google Sheets, Slack, and LinkedIn. Most early stage founders know they should be doing outbound, following up with leads, writing content, qualifying signups, updating spreadsheets, and keeping prospects warm. The problem is that all of this work is repetitive, tool heavy, and easy to drop when you’re also building product and talking to customers. Miniloop lets you hire AI agents that learn your business, connect to your tools, and run those workflows continuously. The goal: help founders and small teams run GTM consistently without hiring more people. Launch video <https://youtu.be/NruQjXiSUZ8 What Miniloop does Miniloop agents can run GTM workflows like: finding and enriching leads writing and sending personalized outbound following up with signups qualifying prospects creating blog and SEO content updating spreadsheets and CRMs sending internal reports and alerts They work across tools like Apollo, Gmail, HubSpot, Google Sheets, LinkedIn, Slack, and more. The goal is simple: give founders and small GTM teams the output of extra teammates without adding headcount. We’ve been using Miniloop internally for our own GTM, including blog generation, lead collection, and signup follow up — workflows that map directly to your current dogfooding setup. Why now Early stage startups used to scale GTM by hiring more people. Now the best teams can stay smaller longer. Instead of duct taping together brittle automations, Miniloop gives you agents that understand context, run multi step workflows, and ask for help only when needed. Who it’s for Miniloop is especially useful if you’re: a founder doing marketing / gtm work yourself a small team without dedicated GTM headcount running outbound, content, lead routing, or follow up manually spending too much time moving data between tools Ask We’re working closely with YC companies and doing hands on workflow setup for early users. If you’re running GTM or ops and want help automating the repetitive parts, happy to set it up with you. Also grateful for intros to teams that are doing lots of manual GTM work across too many tools.

records.description: Soren develops private AI infrastructure for teams in regulated industries. We make it possible for organizations to adopt modern AI without worrying about data privacy or security.
records.launchTitle: Soren - Private AI for Regulated Industries
records.tagline: AI infrastructure built for the world’s most sensitive data.
records.tags: Finance, B2B, Enterprise, AI
records.launchBodyText: Hi 👋 I’m Kevin (https://www.linkedin.com/in/kevinxie06) (MIT), Founder & CEO of Soren (https://soren ai.com). TL;DR Companies today are pushing for AI adoption, but regulated industries often can’t due to privacy and security constraints. Soren enables them to run private, custom AI systems entirely within their own environment. Watch our launch: (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=UZkkMg3pT4Q) ❌ The Problem Companies in regulated industries (e.g. finance, healthcare, and legal services) are under pressure to adopt AI, but strict data protection and compliance requirements make it difficult . Larger enterprises work around this by building internal engineering teams or working directly with the big AI labs. Smaller institutions don’t have that option , leaving them unable to adopt AI or limiting them to only a few use cases. In some cases, they push for adoption while downplaying security and privacy controls. ✅ The Solution Soren develops private AI for companies in regulated industries. We deploy AI models within their own infrastructure and adapt them to their internal data, context, and standards, turning general intelligence into systems that are actually useful in day to day work. Our approach is security first, and we work shoulder to shoulder with clients to ensure data privacy, regulatory compliance, and operational security across all environments. We prioritize small to mid sized institutions, ranging from community and regional banks to boutique law firms. Behind the scenes, we’ve developed a proprietary suite of AI agents and automations that make use remarkably productive, drastically reducing the time and cost of each engagement. The Ask If you are, or you know any of the following, I’d love to hear from you: CTO / CISO / CIO You are the technology leader at a company in a regulated industry and are evaluating whether this AI solution is right for you. Does it deliver on its promise? How would it fit into your day to day operations? Let’s talk. Operations / Business Leaders (COO, Head of Operations, etc.) You’re responsible for improving efficiency and outcomes across your organization. You care about the measurable real world impact of AI solutions in day to day operations. We’d love to show you: Reach out directly to us at kevin@soren ai.com (mailto:kevin@soren ai.com) or book a call here (https://cal.com/kevin soren ai/demo) to learn more!

records.description: Saffron allows companies to quickly evaluate how well software engineers use AI to code. The way engineers write code has fundamentally changed, but the way companies evaluate them hasn't. Saffron gives teams full visibility into how candidates use AI agents to build, debug, and ship real features on an actual codebase. Saffron was founded by Robert Chondro (formerly MIT and Jane Street), Jerry Yao (Stanford and Jane Street), and Kazuma Choji (Harvey Mudd, published in NeurIPS and ICML), and is backed by Y Combinator (P26) and Afore Capital.
records.launchTitle: Saffron: Find Your Next 10x Engineer
records.tagline: Companies use Saffron to evaluate how well software engineers use AI to code.
records.tags: SaaS, Human Resources, HR Tech
records.launchBodyText: TL;DR: Saffron gives candidates a real codebase and ability to use any AI tooling they'd use on the job. We capture every prompt, diff, and decision in a sandboxed environment that gets scored automatically. Teams can now get structured metrics on how candidates actually build, not just whether the final output compiles. Book an onboarding call here: https://trysaffron.ai (https://trysaffron.ai) Our Launch Video <https://youtu.be/pJHXqTKKMRQ The Problem AI coding tools have finally gotten good enough to actually change how engineers work. But the way companies evaluate SWEs hasn't changed at all. Now, interviews are testing the wrong thing and candidates are passing every round of your hiring process, before showing up on the job unable to build a single feature without AI doing the thinking for them. On the flip side, companies are missing out on engineers who actually know how to use AI to be ten times more efficient. We know because we've been on both sides. As candidates, we realized how how much potential there was to become 10x with AI. As founders, we’ve heard the same story from dozens of teams: "They crushed the interview. Two weeks in, they couldn't ship anything independently." Our Approach We’ve built an assessment that embraces AI and measures how efficient an engineer is. Candidates get a real codebase, AI tools they’d actually use on the job, and a task that simulates engineering work. We capture the full process and evaluate it automatically. ⚙️ How It Works Step 1: Create an assessment in under 5 minutes. Point us at your GitHub repo or choose a question from our bank, configure AI agents, and invite candidates. Step 2: Candidates build in a fully managed environment. They get an online IDE that they can customize, a timer, and a real task. No setup required on their end. After coding, they answer debrief questions about their own implementation — and we verify their answers. Step 3: Review results without reading a single line of code. AI review agents score the submission across dimensions you configure with specific evidence from the codebase. We show you detailed metrics like prompts, AI reliance percentage, or tool calling, and give a deterministic score on how well the candidate did. Why Us We’re Robert, Jerry, and Kazuma. We met in high school, started building together, and have been close friends ever since. Robert studied at MIT and was at Jane Street. Jerry studied at Stanford and was also at Jane Street. Kazuma studied at Harvey Mudd and was published in NeurIPS and ICML. All three of us were CS students recruiting for SWE jobs the entirety of our first semester of college. And after noticing how much our coding workflow had changed with the introduction of better and better models, we knew that assessments had to catch up. Our Ask Hiring SWEs? We'll set up your first assessment for free, configure it to your codebase, and work with you 1:1 to build out whatever you need. We're early and hungry. Book a call: https://trysaffron.ai (https://trysaffron.ai) Know an engineering lead, head of talent, or recruiter who's frustrated with their current process? We'd love an intro. Can't wait to hear your feedback! \ Robert, Jerry, and Kazuma

records.description: Salesgraph equips reps with the most relevant context throughout the deal stage, providing proactive pre-call notes and post-call next steps. Sellers are CEOs of their deals; they need to know who the buyer is, what triggered them to take the first call, what their pain points are, how much value this new tool is bringing, are there any technical blockers, etc. A sale is only good when it doesn't feel like a sale to your customer, but rather the sales rep solved a problem for the customer. This was the problem Ricardo and I solved at Mintlify. We created a business case generator that took in call recordings, background news triggers and job updates, Mintlify's ROI and Pricing calculator, past deals won, and customer case studies --> produced a 3 page document expressing the value Mintlify provided + why the quote presented is justifiable. In doing so we closed more enterprise deals at a higher ACV as our champion was able to build a better purchasing case internally. Our goal is to empower every sales team to close deals at a higher ACV, while making sure reps learn from their mistakes and create a smooth sales experience for their end customer.
records.launchTitle: Salesgraph - your marketers, analysts, & strategists, working 24/7 on every deal.
records.tagline: Automate context gathering, collateral, and strategy for enterprise sales teams
records.tags: SaaS, B2B, Sales
records.launchBodyText: Hey we’re Ruhan (https://www.linkedin.com/in/ruhanponnada/) and Ricardo (https://www.linkedin.com/in/ricardonunez 01/) engineer turned seller, and seller turned engineer. Offer: Get a free GTM Audit (https://www.salesgraph.com/gtm audit)on your company or prospects + if you intro us to a closer, for a meeting booked, get free Lulu merch!\ \ TL;DR Salesgraph automates deal context and collateral for enterprise sales teams, so every rep walks into every call prepared and leaves every call with clear next steps. We're a headless UI, starting with a Slack native experience that integrates with your CRM, calendar, call recordings, docs, blogs, case studies, and research. <https://youtu.be/fO6c2wIPwIw \ The Problem: Sales reps have too much on their plate: back to back meetings, Slack messages from prospects, follow up emails, and coordination with SEs. The best reps balance this. The rest forget to follow up, come to calls unprepared, and lose deals at pricing because they can't value sell.\ \ This gets harder when you're selling a technical product as a non technical rep. You can't articulate value if you don't fully understand what you're selling, so reps default to features, line items, and discounts.\ \ The Result: enterprise deals die in procurement. \ \ When reps walk in without a clear business case, procurement has all the leverage. Deals stall on price, ACV gets discounted away, or the deal dies entirely. We lived this at Mintlify. Reps looked great on paper, but folded under pressure. The truth is, sales is as much nurturing as it is selling. The best reps act like consultants, not order takers.\ \ Our Solution: At an internal Mintlify hackathon, our Head of Sales paired with Ricardo to build an internal tool: a value case generator trained on call recordings, past deals, case studies, ROI calculators, and the MEDDIC framework. The output was a 3 page business case our champions used to sell Mintlify internally — driving higher ACVs and helping us close millions from enterprise logos. Why can’t we evangelize every GTM team to close deals at higher ACV through encouraging value selling? \ \ We took those learnings to build Salesgraph. In Slack, reps get proactive context 5 minutes before every call — triggers from background agents, key notes from previous calls, and what to cover next. After every call, they get a DM with strengths, gaps to address, and next steps. We integrate with major call recorders and ship our own ambient recorder (a sales focused alternative to Granola), free for founders and teams under 3 .\ \ Why this matters for everyone, not just reps: Sales managers query deal state directly in Slack, no more reading low quality opp notes or meaningless health scores SEs and FDEs stop watching call recordings at 2x speed; they just ask in Slack Customer success sees what was actually promised vs delivered \ Every rep should sell like your best rep. \ \ Try us out <https://www.salesgraph.com/ .

records.description: ReasonBlocks makes AI agents more accurate and cheaper by catching failures mid-run and compounding reasoning patterns across every agent you deploy. Plugs into your existing agent stack in minutes and starts improving results from the first call.
records.launchTitle: ReasonBlocks - Stop your AI agents from burning money re-learning what they already know.
records.tagline: Infra layer for smarter and cheaper AI agents
records.tags: Artificial Intelligence, SaaS, B2B
records.launchBodyText: TL;DR: Most production agent failures are repeats: same loops, same dead ends, same wasted tokens. Your agent doesn't learn from any of them. ReasonBlocks is a new runtime that catches failures mid run, compresses what doesn't matter, and builds a private reasoning library that grows with every run. Result: agents that get cheaper and more reliable the more they run. 👋 We're @Sajeev Magesh (https://bookface.ycombinator.com/user/2796290) (ex. Stanford) and @Rohan Vij (https://bookface.ycombinator.com/user/3490183) (ex. CMU). We've been building together for 11 years (since 2nd grade!). The Problem Production AI agents fail in patterns. The same loops, the same redundant tool calls, the same second guessing, happening across every customer's deployment, every day. Your agent's traces have all the information needed to prevent recurrence. Almost no system captures it. This means: You're paying for the same failures repeatedly. Token bills grow without agents getting better. Trajectories are inconsistent, your agent re solves the same kind of task differently each time. No compounding improvement: your 10,000th run is no smarter than your 1st. What We Built ReasonBlocks sits in your agent runtime and does three things: 1. Catches failures mid run. Six monitors watch every agent call for loops, redundant work, and second guessing. When one fires, a correction gets injected before tokens are wasted. 2. Compresses what doesn't matter. Stale tool outputs and redundant context get cut from the message history automatically. Your context stays focused, your bill stays small. 3. Captures every lesson into a private reasoning library. After each run, the system extracts what worked and adds it to your library. Patterns get matched and injected into future runs automatically. Your library is yours, built from your production data, growing every day. This isn't just per task memory. It's a runtime that turns every agent run into something the next run can use. Integrate with one command. Works with any model, any framework. Results On SWE bench Pro (75 problems, Sonnet 4.6): 42% accuracy gain 52% token reduction with combined monitor + compression stack 70% fewer budget cap hits Our Asks 1. Try it out — our platform and SDK are live at reasonblocks.com (http://reasonblocks.com). 2. Intros welcome — if you or anyone you know is building vertical AI agents (legal, finance, healthcare, security, research) running at scale, we'd love to chat. 3. Feedback — we're early and learning fast. Tell us what's broken, what's missing, what you'd need to deploy this in production. DM either of us anytime!

records.description: Landeed is the AI-native digital infrastructure for property intelligence, verification, and transactions in India. We provide read/write rails that transform fragmented property and regulatory systems into continuously verified, machine-interpretable state. Powered by Terra, our proprietary property intelligence engine, Landeed converts messy, multi-source records into structured, queryable knowledge that supports legal-grade due diligence, collateral verification, title search, transfers, and property payments. With coverage across 24 states, enterprises go live nationally from day one. We are trusted by Fortune India 500 companies across BFSI, real estate, and retail. Landeed is the blue tick for property.
records.launchTitle: 🏠 Terra: Turning Indian property from a who-you-know market into a what-you-know market.
records.tagline: Indian property, finally legible.
records.tags: Documents, Artificial Intelligence, Fintech, Legal, Proptech
records.launchBodyText: Today we're launching Terra: the thing we've wanted to build since day one.. If you caught our original launch: <https://www.ycombinator.com/launches/GxK landeed do you own your property in india are you sure let s check you know we've spent three years building India's property infrastructure. More than a million people use Landeed monthly searching millions of properties and growing at an increasing pace. The result of that work is Terra India’s first and most intelligent property AI trained on 500M+ title documents. 24 states. 22 languages. Even more importantly, we built Terra on the foundations of our groundwork not guesswork. The answers are backed by verifiable records from regulatory registries and ground truth minimizing hallucination risks. Furthermore, this is the third major launch from Landeed Labs (our internal AI division) after AITSR our flagship product for enterprises which has now evolved into Terra Black. Check it out below: <https://youtu.be/Zy3XsLzm9vY Here's what's broken. India is two markets stitched together: who you know and what you know . Most of India's economy has shifted toward what you know over the last twenty years. Real estate has not. It is the most extreme who you know market in the country. Who you know determines what you can buy, what price you pay, what risks you discover, and whether your title holds up in court. If you have the right cousin in the registrar's office, the right lawyer in the right city, the right broker who owes you a favor — you have edge. If you don't, you're flying blind with your life savings. This is why India has $10 trillion in property and most of it is dead capital — Hernando de Soto's phrase. You own it but you can't prove it. You inherit it but you can't read it. You want to buy it but you can't verify it. Many call this dead capital but at Landeed we call this just another Tuesday. The data was always there. It was just illegible, scattered across 28 state systems, locked in 22 languages, and impossible to reason about. Here's what we built. Terra is the first product that makes data competitive with relationships in Indian property — and wins. You just talk to it. In English, Hindi, Telugu, Tamil — whatever you think in. "Find me 2BHK apartments in Gachibowli under 1 crore and with nice gym." Or drop the link or url from your favorite listing to explore more about the property in detail like guideline value or average rentals. Terra shows you real listings across all aggregated from listings platforms, helps you create watchlists, calculate price trends and check property risks all in one screen in real time in one conversation. "Who has owned that property since it was built?" Ownership tree. Every transaction. Linked to source documents you can open. "Are there any encumbrances or legal issues?" Light diligence in minutes. Not a lawyer's 2 week report. "Compare this to the one in Kondapur I was looking at yesterday." Side by side. Under the hood: a system of specialized AI agents working in parallel. Live integrations with 24 state governments. Proprietary OCR for 22 Indian languages including handwritten records from the 1980s. Terra doesn't guess. It reads the actual government records — the same ones a lawyer would read — and reasons about them in real time. One moment that shows what this unlocks. A user told Terra about his property next to a biryani shop in Chennai. Terra pulled the documents and found a municipal announcement from that week — the user was now eligible to build additional square footage on his existing plot. He didn't know to ask. Terra found it anyway. That's the point. Terra doesn't just answer questions. It tells you things you didn't know to ask. Early traction. Soft launched on web a month ago. 50,000 users showed up without a rupee of paid acquisition. Try it: https://web.landeed.com/terra (https://web.landeed.com/terra?thread=a3c27682 da8c 462a 8855 5d2ebcd80632) or download our app from play store or iOS What we want from the community: Know anyone buying, selling, or inheriting property in India? Send them to Terra. Especially NRIs — they have the worst version of this problem. Know a bank, NBFC, or real estate operator in India? We have Terra Black (enterprise API) already running with HDFC Bank, Hero FinCorp, Amazon India, Vastu Housing, and others. Warm intros welcome: sanjay@landeed.com (mailto:sanjay@landeed.com) Feedback, ideas, critiques — send them straight to us. India's property market has been a who you know game since the British left. Terra is how it finally becomes a what you know market. We make this inevitable. 🐂

records.description: Asendia AI builds AI recruiters for staffing agencies and enterprise companies. It clones their best recruiters into AI agents that match, screen, and submit candidates 10x faster.
records.launchTitle: Asendia AI — Clone Your Best Recruiter!
records.tagline: Asendia AI builds AI recruiters for staffing agencies and enterprise companies. It clones their best recruiters into AI agents that match, screen, and submit candidates 10x faster.
records.tags: Artificial Intelligence, Recruiting, AI
records.launchBodyText: We are Rihab Lajmi (https://www.linkedin.com/in/rihab l 085182164/) and Badis Zormati, (https://www.linkedin.com/in/badis zormati/) Co founders of Asendia AI. (https://asendia.ai/) Asendia AI clones your best recruiters into AI agents that match, screen, and submit candidates 10x faster. When a new role comes in, our agents search your own database, look at inbound applicants and reach out over phone, SMS, and WhatsApp, run screening conversations, and deliver ranked shortlists with written submittals, all modeled on how your top recruiters actually work. Your team gets their time back for the only part that actually needs them: closing candidates, working closely with clients and building relationships. Our Launch Video: <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=8pdqUklre6o The problem Staffing agencies lose placements every day, not because they lack talent, but because their recruiters don't have enough hours to place candidates in time. The average recruiter spends 80% of their day on manual redundant work. Screening, data entry, outreach, follow ups, scheduling. The actual recruiting, the judgment, the relationships, the conversations that close, gets two hours a day if they're lucky. Every hour spent on admin is a candidate they didn't submit. Every candidate they didn't submit is a placement a faster agency took. In staffing, speed isn't a nice to have. It's the entire margin. What we built On the Asendia platform, you build your own AI recruiters. Each one can be cloned from how your best human recruiters actually work, their screening criteria, their outreach style, their prioritization logic. You configure the workflows: which channels they use (phone, SMS, WhatsApp, email), how they screen, what qualifies a candidate, when they escalate to a human. Under the hood, Asendia connects to your ATS and digests every candidate record, every client req, every placement history. Your AI recruiters don't search by keywords. They understand your database the way a tenured recruiter does, matching on real context, flagging candidates based on signals like contract end dates, availability shifts, and past behavior. Every morning, your team gets a brief: who was sourced, who responded, who's qualified, and what needs attention. Your human recruiters stop spending eight hours on admin and start spending eight hours on the part that wins placements: judgment, relationships, and closing. The team AI Recruiting B2B artificial intelligence Hiring Agents Agentic

records.description: Dayjob builds autonomous AI workers for transport operations. Our scheduling agent plugs into existing ERPs and continuously re-optimises routes in real time - handling new jobs, driver changes, and exceptions automatically. Customers in waste management see 8%+ efficiency gains from day one.
records.launchTitle: Dayjob: AI Agents for Industrial Logistics
records.tagline: Meet Dayjob, AI Scheduling for Waste Trucks
records.launchBodyText: Hey 👋 We're George and Fred, founders of Dayjob! What we solved in the UK and are now solving in the US \ \ Industrial logistics plans are still built manually, every single morning. A transport planner sits down at 7am, spends 60 to 90 minutes dragging jobs onto trucks, and the plan is usually wrong by 10am. Our customers tell us they literally cannot hire and train planners fast enough. It’s a nightmare when one of those planners is off sick or has to go away on vacation. Humans are being asked to continuously re optimise hundreds of constraints: vehicle capacity, weight limits, time windows, driver hours, live traffic, all in real time. It's cognitively impossible to do reliably. If a fleet can complete just 10 more jobs a day, that's worth millions per year. The opportunity cost of bad scheduling is enormous and almost nobody in this industry has solved it. <https://youtu.be/GI2HtwWodpc?si=4GbPN1Uj UnNkDoh What we've built: Dayjob sits on top of existing ERP software. Our scheduling agent builds a full fleet schedule in about 60 seconds. The same task currently takes a human planner most of the morning. We're live with fleets in the UK today, and currently launching in the US. Early results: 5 to 10% improvement in fleet efficiency from day one 50% improvement in hitting customer time slots One customer saw an £800K revenue uplift in their first year We started in waste management deliberately. It's one of the hardest planning environments in existence: high variability, tight regulatory rules, constant real time changes. If you can solve scheduling there, you can solve it anywhere in industrial logistics. The backstory: Fred and I met at Oxford 13 years ago. In 2022 we started building ERP software for waste operators. We spent early mornings in depots watching planners work, rode in trucks, sat next to schedulers as they built their routes. We pivoted to Dayjob in January 2025. We're now at $496K ARR, growing fast, and scheduling is just our first agent. We have three more covering the full operational workflow launching later this year. We'd love to connect with: Anyone in the waste hauling market or the broader industrial logistics market. Let’s go 🚛

records.launchBodyText: Hey YC, we're Ethan (https://www.linkedin.com/in/ethan kinnan) and Norbu (https://www.linkedin.com/in/norbusonam) from Sherpa. TL;DR Sherpa turns more website visitors into customers by running experiments on its own. Here’s how: → Sherpa studies visitor behavior and finds opportunities to increase your site’s conversion → It launches on brand experiments without a designer or developer → Winners ship and your site’s conversion rate goes up \ Offer: Get a free review of your website from Sherpa in 30 seconds. Here’s the link. (https://www.withsherpa.ai/?utm source=yc launch) \ Meet Ryan. Ryan runs growth marketing at Lindy. After installing Sherpa he shipped 40 website experiments in 30 days without a designer, web developer, or engineer. These weren't just headline or copy changes. Experiments included full layout changes, animations, onboarding, and even pricing. To date, total site conversion is up 30%. We sent a film crew to Ryan’s office, watch below: \ <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RNb2WkElu2M \ The problem Your website is the highest leverage asset your company owns. Every visitor you've ever paid for lands on it. And most of them leave. Every growth team knows their site could convert better. Conversion rate optimization (CRO) is a volume game but each experiment needs a data analyst, a designer, a web developer, and someone to call winners. Most marketing teams don't have that bench. The ones that do still only ship 2 3 experiments a month because coming up with ideas, building them, and coordinating handoffs takes weeks. Experiments that could double your business are out there, but getting them live today feels like this: \ How Sherpa works Instead of waiting weeks per experiment, you can install Sherpa. It comes up with the ideas, builds the experiments, and you approve which ones go live with one click. Sherpa is your conversion rate optimization team compressed into one line of code. \ \ It studies how your visitors behave and compares your funnel to competitors to find opportunities to increase conversion. Then it designs on brand variants, ships them, and runs A/B tests to identify winners. Sherpa can even spin up a new landing page for every ad, so each visitor lands on a page matched to the exact content they clicked. The longer Sherpa runs, the more it learns how to improve conversion on your site.\ \ Our customers ship dozens of experiments a month without adding more work for their team. Here are two examples. \ Lindy Sherpa noticed the problem section had the highest dwell time and dropoff on the page. The feature cards were close behind. Both sections were bottom three in stop and convert rate. Sherpa removed the problem section and replaced the static feature cards with a new collapsed section that took up less space. Before: After: Gauge Sherpa found that visitors who read their Eco case study booked demos 2.5x more often. It also noticed visitors engaging with the FAQ were consistently clicking on the 2 questions trying to better understand what AEO is. Sherpa shipped a new animated hero explaining AEO and added a case study callout under the primary CTA. Before: After: \ Who we are Norbu (CTO) : shipped AI products at Microsoft before leading agent infrastructure and growth engineering at Lindy, where he ran 50+ growth experiments and tripled paywall conversion. Ethan (CEO) : was Lindy’s first forward deployed engineer and taught 10,000+ people to build custom agents, live. Automated every job he's had with AI since the GPT 3 API launched. We’re obsessed with self improving systems. Websites and conversion are just the start. We’re building toward a world where software continuously optimizes for whoever is using it — human or agent. Ask: → Own a website? Have Sherpa review your site for free in 30 seconds here (https://www.withsherpa.ai/?utm source=yc launch) → Want to talk? Book a call (https://cal.com/team/sherpa/demo?utm source=yc launch) → Know a marketing or e commerce leader whose website changes take forever and would benefit from a 20% conversion bump? Do them a favor and send them this 🤝 Thanks YC. — Ethan & Norbu

records.description: Today, when a city gets a FOIA request, the process is still painfully manual. City clerks have to hunt through archived emails, meeting minutes, PDFs, and shared drives just to find the relevant records. Many offices still manually black out sensitive information with a Sharpie, line by line. Local governments spend billions processing these requests. It’s slow, expensive, and a large request can create massive backlogs. If a city misses the deadline for a request, they can be sued for failing to comply with public records law. GovGuard uses AI agents to automatically search across years of records, flag sensitive information for redaction, and prepare draft response letters for review. Our redaction engine handles harder edge cases like trade secrets and privileged material. Legacy tools require clerks to manually cite state codes for every redaction, while GovGuard is built with these exemptions. The new surge of AI-generated requests are overwhelming clerk offices. Most cities we speak with are seeing request volume grow 2x year over year, with no increase in staff. The tools they have today are basically Jira. They help manage the ticket/request, but not complete the work.

records.launchBodyText: TLDR: Huscarl is an AI native insurance advisory firm for corporations ($50M+ revenue). We help them save 30% on annual premiums with zero downside. https://youtu.be/HuHWqokdRFo (https://youtu.be/HuHWqokdRFo) Hey guys, we’re Paulien and Alex, and we’re building Huscarl to fix corporate insurance. records.description: Short-term rental operators suffer from diseconomies of scale. As listing count grows, operational complexity increases exponentially, managers lose control, reviews drop, and so do rates and homeowner retention. Most are stuck between 10 and 50 listings, using 5 to 10 different software tools, none flexible enough for their needs. There is no unified data layer of their company. They navigate in the dark, reacting to problems rather than delivering hospitality. Trellis unifies everything into one platform, giving AI agents complete context over operations (communications, tasks, workforce, smart homes, bookings, accounting, revenue management) so they can orchestrate work end to end. We believe software is being commoditized. We reached feature parity with competitors in two weeks. If agents can build software that fast, then every SaaS product becomes a software builder. The vendor provides the trellis: the fixed infrastructure. The AI grows the vines: dynamic features, workflows, and interfaces that adapt to each customer. No more adapting to the tool. The tool adapts to you. But the real value is not in what the agent does for one company. It is in what it learns across all of them. Every workflow that works, every SOP that survives contact with guests, every resolution pattern that prevents a bad review feeds back into the network. The agent shares best practices, battle proven SOPs, and guest intelligence across companies. This creates a network of property management brands where the experience is designed by humans and ensured by software. Quality becomes predictable. Scale becomes unlimited. Hospitality is no longer a people problem, it is a software problem. Airbnb built the marketplace. We are building the machine that runs it.
records.launchTitle: Trellis: Autonomous Agents for Short Term Rentals
records.tagline: Run all your operations end to end across every tool in your stack.
records.launchBodyText: Hi, we’re Lodovico Benvenuti (https://www.linkedin.com/in/lodovico benvenuti 11678224a/) and Jan Sahagun (https://www.linkedin.com/in/jan sahagun escosa?utm source=share&utm campaign=share via&utm content=profile&utm medium=ios app), and we’re building Trellis, AI agents that run operations for short term rental property managers. Our Launch Video: <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Zki0 icE3Xw The problem Short term rental operations break as companies scale. As you add listings, coordination explodes across guest messaging, cleaning, maintenance, and homeowners. Work ends up spread across 5 to 10 disconnected tools, duck taped together by burnt out teams, constantly firefighting issues, with information inevitably falling through the cracks. Quality drops, reviews follow, and growth stalls. This is because managing a property is complex. Let’s say the guest calls about a broken shower. The operator has to identify the reservation in the PMS, search a separate knowledge base for context, collect photos, and create a task in another system. Then check a workforce tool for availability, reprioritize tasks, dispatch a technician, follow up with the guest, and later log costs, update accounting, and notify the homeowner. All this across 5 10 systems of record. The core issue is simple: no software has full operation context to control operations end to end. What we built Trellis provides AI agents that run operations across the entire stack. The agent communicates with guests, dispatches and coordinates cleaners and maintenance, files claims and costs, and follows up until issues are fully resolved. It connects to PMSs, messaging channels, and internal tools, but more importantly, it maintains full operational context: who the guest is, what property they’re in, what the issue is, what’s already been tried, who is available, what tasks are in progress, and what needs to happen next. Instead of passing information between tools, the agent owns the entire workflow from first message to resolution and reporting. When agents take over operations, execution becomes consistent and verifiable. That shifts control of the guest experience from marketplaces back to operators, who can now deliver reliable service at scale, build real brands, and own demand. Our background Before Trellis, Lodo built AI customer service agents for short term rentals at Conduit (YC W24). He also scaled a property management company to \ $2M revenue and 150+ listings. With trellis we are now running their operations with 1 person. Jan spent years in the trenches of the STR industry: leading early sales at multiple AI tools for property managers, building an STR tech media platform (STR Tech Report), consulting across the space, attending 20+ industry events, and speaking with thousands of property managers along the way. That journey earned him deep trust and relationships throughout the STR world. If you run a property management company, we’d love to show you Trellis.

records.description: The product detail page is one of the last unchanged surfaces in commerce. Brands have spent years getting sophisticated at driving traffic there but nobody fixed what happens when shoppers arrive. Kinect is the AI shopping assistant layer for e-commerce. We plug into ecommerce stores and give agents live access to your catalog, inventory, and policies. A brand’s online sales rep that can answer all a customer’s questions, help them find the right products, convince them. The website itself adapts to the shopper’s context: it considers the ads the user clicked on, their searches, filters, scrolling behavior, chat context to all show the shopper what they need to see. We're building for human shoppers today and the AI agents that will shop for them tomorrow. Either way, the product page needs to get a lot smarter. That's Kinect – the product page finally does what it was supposed to do all along.

records.launchBodyText: \ \ \ \ TLDR: Kinect is a conversational commerce layer for E Commerce brands. We turn static product pages into adaptive, intelligent experiences that actually help shoppers buy. 8 design partners, seeing 20% conversion lifts and 30% ROAS improvements Go check us out live on one of our favorite customers' stores with a discount code: KINECT10 sousasupps.com (http://sousasupps.com) Problem E Commerce websites haven't changed in years. Most have converged to the same layout with the same standard information. Meanwhile, everything around them has changed – consumers get personalized feeds on social media, AI platforms to talk to, hyper personalized ads. But where do they go to actually spend their money? Same static website as everyone else. Brands can't answer questions, help customers find the right product, or adapt to context. They don't sell, they display. What's Kinect Kinect is the conversational layer for e commerce – built for human shoppers today and AI shopping agents tomorrow. We plug into E Commerce stores and give agents live access to your catalog, inventory, and policies. Each brand’s online sales rep can answer any customer question, help them find the right products, convince them . The website itself adapts to the shopper’s context: it considers the ads the user clicked on, their searches, filters, scrolling behavior, chat context to show the shopper what they need to see. Think of it as giving every product page your best sales rep. Launch Video: https://youtu.be/fSOVQKz8AVM (https://youtu.be/fSOVQKz8AVM) Early Traction 8 design partners across Wellness, Fashion, Sporting Goods, Consumer Goods Seeing up to 20% conversion lifts in early deployments 40% more engaged customers vs. standard PDPs Seeing up to 30% improved ROAS Backstory Kratik and I spent the past year at Reevo building conversational intelligence for B2B sales – extracting buyer intent from dialogue, helping enterprise teams understand what prospects actually wanted, and close more deals faster. As we built for B2B reps, our E Commerce friends kept asking for something like this for their own brands – a way to reimagine the website to drive conversion and personalization. So we got to work. Asks If you know someone who runs an E Commerce brand (or you are one) – we’d like to give them early access + dinner on us for you! We're looking for: Founders, marketing, and E Commerce leaders at D2C brands Agencies and Vendors experts who work with merchants Reach out on Bookface or kratik@trykinect.com (mailto:kratik@trykinect.com) + varun@trykinect.com (mailto:varun@trykinect.com) Thanks YC!

records.launchBodyText: Hey everyone 🙂 We’re Tim (https://www.linkedin.com/in/timzinkl/) and Matthias (https://www.linkedin.com/in/matthias wolf/), co founders of Pairio TL:DR: Machine downtime in manufacturing is expensive. Pairio helps technicians and operators resolve faults faster with voice AI. 4 plants in Germany are already using Pairio to reduce equipment downtime. Ask: Can you introduce us to plant and maintenance managers at US manufacturing firms? Contact us at tim.zinkl@pairio.com (mailto:tim.zinkl@pairio.com) <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=kmUBOkAU33M The Problem Unplanned equipment downtime costs global industries over $1.4 trillion every year – and the root cause isn't broken machines. It's broken knowledge transfer. When a machine fails, resolution speed depends almost entirely on whether the one guy who's seen this before is working that day Valuable technical clues are buried somewhere across 400 pages of OEM manuals or locked in outdated software After spending 6 hours getting the line back up, the last thing a technician does is sit down and write a detailed fault report to institutionalize their knowledge With the average technician in the US now 50 years old, knowledge is literally walking out the door, further compounding the problem for years to come. Our Solution We have shown at 4 factories in Germany that removing these barriers can lead to higher equipment uptime – and the effect compounds with every fault processed with Pairio. With our voice first approach we empower technicians and operators to: Simply ask and instantly receive any available technical clue Speak and let Pairio structure their learnings into reusable know how Use photos and videos to capture even more contextual knowledge By focusing on capturing the reality on the shop floor we are building the foundation for agentic workflows that eliminate off the wrench time and optimize equipment uptime. The Ask If you know anyone in plant operations or maintenance in US manufacturing we’d love to talk with them – happy to hook you up with some of Munich’s finest beers as a gesture of gratitude. The Team We met 4 years ago at an entrepreneurship scholarship from the Technical University of Munich. Matthias worked as the first engineer at Lio (YC S23) and Tim as the second employee at the battery recycling startup tozero.

records.description: We build AI scientists that discover new materials for the semiconductor industry - specifically datacenters and fabs. Finding novel materials today takes 10+ years of lab work. We aim to compress that timeline to months, using a swarm of AI agents. Akash completed his PhD at Stanford on material discovery for semiconductors. The materials he discovered for nanoscale interconnects have been adopted into the roadmaps of Intel and TSMC. Advaith was the founding applied scientist at Persona AI (acquired by Luma Labs), where he built long horizon autonomous agents for major telecom and crypto companies. Power consumption and heat release from AI chips is doubling every year. The semiconductor industry needs new materials to keep this exponential going - Matforge will help find them.
records.launchTitle: Matforge - AI Scientists to discover new semiconductor materials
records.tagline: Discovering 10x better materials for use in next generation electronic chips
records.tags: Artificial Intelligence, Advanced Materials, Semiconductors, AI
records.launchBodyText: Accelerating scientific discovery Tl:dr We’re building AI scientists to discover new materials for the semiconductor industry. If you know Packaging/thermal engineers at chip companies like NVIDIA, AMD, Groq, Cerebras, Google, Intel, Samsung, TSMC etc, please put us in touch! — Discovering a material with commercial viability is usually a story of persistence. The incandescent lightbulb filament came not from a single guess, but from Edison trying 6000+ candidate materials over 20 years. The first transistor ever made in Bell labs was famously not with silicon , but germanium. (left) The first transistor, made from Germanium. (right) some materials Edison tried as lightbulb filaments The same holds true in the semiconductor industry. We really need better materials for our chips, but finding them takes 10+ years of lab work. At Matforge, we build AI agents to accelerate this process of finding new materials for the semiconductor industry. Our agents work across the entire materials discovery process coming up with new candidates, synthesizing them and testing them in physical labs. The founders Advaith has a Masters in AI from Carnegie Mellon , and was most recently a research engineer at Luma Labs, building agent harnesses and working on frontier models. Akash has a Masters, PhD and PostDoc from Stanford University in materials discovery for the semiconductor industry. We’ve known each for the last 11 years , and have even written a book together! Breakthrough materials will still take many, many experiments. But for the first time, a small team might just have the tools to speed up this search and win.

records.launchBodyText: tldr: LLMs are built for human in the loop tasks which are highly non deterministic in nature. We built an AI model for tasks that require high accuracy and verifiable data you can build workflows around. Interfaze is an AI model built on a new architecture that combines specialized DNN+CNN models with transformers for developer tasks that require deterministic output and high consistency like: Vision (OCR, Object detection, GUI) Structured web extraction Audio (STT, Diarization, Audio semantics) Classification (Image, Text) Web search and more Try here: <https://interfaze.ai If you’ve ever tried to build a production application using LLMs for tasks like OCR, web scraping, or strict structured data extraction, you already know the pain: hallucinated keys, broken JSON, hidden inaccurate data and massive latency spikes. You usually end up chaining multiple open source models together just to get a reliable result but face challenges with scaling or being stuck with outdated models from providers like AWS, Azure and GCP. Model comparison Check out the latest benchmarks on our site here (https://interfaze.ai). OCR example for KYC Beyond higher accuracy, now you get additional data that allows you to both validate your data and build reliable pipelines as a developer. A subset of the additional metadata you get when you run an OCR task 👇 Note: the confidence score value can be used to build verifiable systems. bounding boxes can be used to trace items for consistent tasks with guessing. Web scraping for LinkedIn We all know how hard it is to scrape sites like LinkedIn, whether you are switching proxy providers, rewriting your scripts, or even getting your IP banned. Interfaze is trained to work with the browser infra beyond just looking at pure html like traditional LLMs. Allowing it to learn new work around, rotate proxies when needed and figure out how to scrape any site under 30 seconds. GUI/Computer use Filling a form Object Detection Construction site equipment check Architecture We spent a year researching a new architecture to solve this problem. We just couldn’t accept that attention is all you need, we think you need a new interfaze 😉 Full paper: <https://arxiv.org/abs/2602.04101 (Accepted into IEEE CAI 2026) Team I've been a developer and ML engineer for the past 8 years, working with ML models on the edge for real world experiences like motion capture and navigation mapping to backend workflows like OCR, Web scraping pipelines, and more. Socials: LinkedIn (https://www.linkedin.com/in/yoeven/?skipRedirect=true), X (https://x.com/yoeven) Harsha has over 5 years of experience specializing in computer vision, reinforcement learning for SLMs, and AI research with multiple peer reviewed papers. Socials: LinkedIn (https://www.linkedin.com/in/harsha vardhan khurdula/), X (https://x.com/khurdula) Interfaze X: <https://x.com/interfaze ai <https://interfaze.ai/ \ Interfaze LinkedIn: <https://www.linkedin.com/company/interfaze ai Site: https://interfaze.ai (https://interfaze.ai/) Docs: <https://interfaze.ai/docs

records.launchBodyText: Double your AI coding output per dollar spent with WOZCODE WOZCODE (https://www.wozcode.com/) is a plugin that augments Claude Code, making it faster, cheaper and higher performing. It installs in two commands, uses your existing Claude subscription, and fits directly into your current tools and workflows (CLI, Claude Desktop App, VS Code, Conductor, etc.) <https://youtu.be/ISDH4qsLbrM?si=Pm1g7xXYohUX UVH Problem: Last month, our Claude Code spend crossed $100k. The scary part wasn’t the total. It was how much Claude wasted by being inefficient. Tool call after tool call that bloated context and burned unnecessary tokens. Claude Code is incredible, but too often it was wasting our tokens and money. As a startup we couldn’t continue burning funds in this fashion. So we built a fix. Solution: WOZCODE (https://www.wozcode.com/) is a plugin that optimizes how Claude Code works under the hood. Compared to Claude Code alone, WOZCODE delivers: 25–55% cost savings 30–40% faster coding tasks Higher benchmark performance (Terminal Bench: 70% vs 58%) For teams using Claude Code heavily, this can mean: 50% Lower API spend 2x more AI coding throughput 2x more output before hitting limits \ checkout wozcode.com (http://wozcode.com) for side by sides on real world coding tasks How it works Instead of relying solely on the default tools inside Claude Code, WOZCODE uses a set of custom tools designed for efficiency and context optimization. For example, in vanilla Claude Code, a simple "find and edit 3 files" takes 9+ calls (3× Glob/Grep + 3× Read + 3× Edit) — and call 9 reprocesses all prior output as input tokens.\ \ WOZCODE collapses that to 2 calls (1× Search + 1× batch Edit). The context stays small, so every subsequent call in the session is cheaper. Over a 30 prompt session, this compounding effect drives significant cost savings. See what you could be saving: Run the below common in your terminal. The sooner we learn about your use case the sooner we can optimize for it and help you save. Reach us at founders@withwoz.com (mailto:founders@withwoz.com) Thank you! \ Ben & Brad

records.description: CatchAll by NewsCatcher is a recall-first web search API built for queries where the results are spread across hundreds or thousands of pages on the web. Instead of returning the top ranked links like traditional search engines, CatchAll retrieves a large candidate set from the web, validates which pages actually match the query, and extracts structured records of real-world events. Developers and data teams use CatchAll to answer “long-list” questions such as tracking regulatory actions, funding rounds, product launches, corporate expansions, or cybersecurity incidents. The output is not just links but clean, deduplicated datasets that can power AI agents, monitoring systems, analytics pipelines, and market intelligence workflows. CatchAll runs on the data infrastructure developed by NewsCatcher, which continuously indexes millions of articles and public web pages across a global network of sources.
records.launchTitle: CatchAll: Recall-first web search API
records.tagline: Turning the web into a structured database of real-world events
records.tags: Artificial Intelligence, SaaS, Enterprise, Big Data, Enterprise Software
records.launchBodyText: Hello, hackers! We’re Artem and Maksym, founders of NewsCatcher.\ \ We’re building a structured, event centric index of what happens in the real world. Today, that starts with CatchAll — a recall first search API that finds all relevant matches to a query, extracts structured facts, and delivers clean, ready to use datasets.\ \ <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=GPfVdNRoDsA \ \ The problem \ Traditional web search — and most search APIs — were designed for humans. They optimize for speed and ranking , which works well when one page contains the answer: “Who is the CEO of a company?” “What’s the latest acquisition in X?” But many high stakes questions don’t have a single answer: All regulatory actions affecting an industry Every cybersecurity incident in a given week All funding rounds, policy changes, or facility expansions In these cases, recall is everything .\ If 200 valid events exist and your system surfaces 5, your recall is 2.5%. No amount of prompting or post processing fixes that. LLMs and “deep research agents” haven’t solved this. They still open and read pages one by one, hit context limits, and end up sampling rather than exhausting a topic. \ The solution \ CatchAll is a recall first web search API built for long list answers — answers distributed across the open web. CatchAll: Pulls a large candidate set from NewsCatcher’s proprietary web index (often tens of thousands of pages) Validates which pages actually match the criteria Extracts structured fields (companies, dates, locations, actions, amounts — defined by the user) Normalizes and deduplicates everything into clean records The output isn’t links.\ It’s a dataset that didn’t exist before — and can be kept up to date by turning the query into a live monitor . For teams, the end result is fewer searches and more reliable alerts.\ \ Backstory \ Maksym and I have known each other for almost 20 years. NewsCatcher is our first startup — we've been building it since 2020. It started as a bootstrapped self serve news API for startups, and then grew into custom enterprise solutions (powering the US Department of State, Transparency International, Samsung, 50+ enterprise clients). CatchAll is us coming back to self serve: same infrastructure, now optimized for the AI agent ecosystem.\ \ Sign up here (https://platform.newscatcherapi.com/catchall?utm source=launch yc&utm medium=social&utm campaign=Launch YC CatchAll&utm id=catch all YC), test CatchAll (for free), and give us feedback (we’ll 5x your credits!).\ \ Share your use cases:\ artem@newscatcherapi.com (mailto:artem@newscatcherapi.com) or DM on https://www.linkedin.com/in/artem bugara (https://www.linkedin.com/in/artem bugara/)

records.launchBodyText: TLDR: Cosine is built for the point where coding agents usually start to break down: longer tasks, multiple threads of work, team handoff, and code you still need to maintain later. PS. you can use your Claude Code, OpenAI and Copilot subscriptions (Don’t tell OpenCode or OpenClaw :P ) For OpenAI and Copilot subscriptions: Launch Cosine CLI Ctrl+p and choose ChatGPT or Copilot subscription model You'll get redirected to ChatGPT or Copilot website for auth For Claude: Make sure you have a Claude Pro or Max subscription and have it installed on your local environment Install the Cosine CLI — cosine.sh/cli (mac, windows and Linux supported) Ctrl+p and choose the Claude Max Subscription (it works with both Claude pro and max) Your Claude Code subscription is recognised automatically — Cosine uses the actual Claude Code binary, so no API key needed Run cos in any repo — your Claude subscription quota is used directly, no extra Cosine billing for model usage) <https://youtu.be/F61PCXvXd3Y More info: If you’re already deep into AI coding tools, you probably know the feeling: You start with quick wins in terminal, then pretty soon you want the same speed on bigger tasks, across more than one thread of work, without losing track of what the agent is actually doing or ending up with code your team has to clean up later. That’s basically why we built Cosine 3.0 Cosine is one coding agent across CLI, VS Code, desktop, and cloud. So you can start with a quick local task, pick it up somewhere else later, or spin up parallel agents when the job gets bigger, without hopping between separate tools or losing context. What we kept caring about as we built it was pretty simple: We wanted something that still feels fast and direct, but gives you more visibility and more control once the work stops being a toy problem. So you can inspect changes properly. So you can follow what happened. So you can undo things cleanly. So you can run multiple threads of work when one agent is not enough. So the whole thing still makes sense when other people on your team have to touch the code. A lot of tools feel great right up until you want them to do more than one thing, in more than one place, on code that actually matters. We wanted to build the version that keeps working after that point.
