records.launchBodyText: Regbase helps companies track new laws across the world and at the local level. Regbase is an AI native research platform built to help legal teams, researchers, and government vendors monitor regulatory changes across countries, languages, and hard to find government sources . https://youtu.be/lY1La0A4iaw (https://youtu.be/lY1La0A4iaw?si= dCa9ucc4Sq8jvXR) The problem: Companies that need to track new laws still rely heavily on manual research. Many important updates are posted on obscure local websites, draft bill portals, consultation pages, social media, and other sources that may never get indexed by Google or picked up by LLMs. If a law is not in English, not covered by the media, or it only applies at the local level, it can be impossible to find. \ The solution: Regbase finds these updates automatically, including draft bills, consultations, compliance requirements, and sales opportunities like grants. Then it analyzes them. So far we have: Found $400M in expiring school safety grants across the U.S. for a client selling protective equipment Identified 80 requirements for greenhouse gas disclosure in 45 countries for a global manufacturer Found dozens of obscure laws in non English languages, posted only on LinkedIn, Facebook or X Uncovered regulations hidden behind QR codes, and even one government website that required visitors to watch an ad! Who we are: Natalie Aresta Katz , previously led a law firm practice research team at Paul, Weiss, tracking laws across dozens of countries and every state. Ethan Shea is a former Staff Engineer at Vercel with expertise building agentic infrastructure. They started the company after years of struggling with manual regulatory updates across jurisdictions and languages. The ask: We are eager to help lawyers and researchers in all areas, including employment, AI, climate/sustainability, and private equity. We also work with companies in regulated industries, and state and local government vendors. Visit regbase.com (http://regbase.com) or email us at founders@regbase.com (mailto:founders@regbase.com) to book a time. Follow us: https://www.linkedin.com/company/regbase (https://www.linkedin.com/company/regbase/) | <https://x.com/theregbase

records.description: The first AI data analyst that has its own computer. It sees and understands your docs, Slack, databases, data lakes, and codebase. Query, debug, and analyze right where your team works.
records.launchTitle: Datost - The most accurate AI data analyst
records.tagline: A proactive AI data analyst with a built in semantic layer that actually understands your business
records.tags: B2B, Analytics, AI Assistant
records.launchBodyText: Hey everyone — This is Maceo and Jason, founders of Datost. TL;DR: Datost is an AI data analyst your team @mentions in Slack. It keeps a semantic layer of your business definitions so it understands what questions mean before writing SQL. On the hardest public text to SQL benchmark, it scores 75.2%. The best frontier model alone gets 33%. Why I built this I built Datost while I was at Traba, a staffing marketplace. Traba had a lot of data and not enough analysts. Someone would need a number to make a decision, and the path to that number ran through SQL they couldn’t write or an analyst who was busy. The question would sit for hours. By the time the answer showed up, the decision had already been made on a guess. There are AI analytics tools out there now. They all have the same problem: they don’t understand your business. When your PM asks “which accounts are at risk of churning,” there’s no column called that. “At risk” has a formula that lives in someone’s head, or a doc, or nowhere. These tools guess an interpretation and commit. Then someone in a Slack thread acts on the answer before anyone checks whether it was right. The semantic layer Datost maintains a layer of your team’s definitions, what “churn risk” means at your company, how you calculate NRR, which tables are trustworthy, what that internal acronym stands for. When it hits a term it hasn’t seen, it looks it up the same way a new analyst would ask a coworker. When the semantic layer doesn’t have it either, it asks the user before writing any SQL. It learns from mistakes too. When it gets something wrong, it leaves notes on the schema so it doesn’t make the same mistake twice. Teams that have been using it for a few months barely correct it anymore. What it actually does Your team @mentions Datost in any Slack channel. It searches the semantic layer, explores your schema in a sandbox, writes and tests SQL, then posts the answer in thread with the work shown. A second model reviews every answer read only. If the definition says one thing and the SQL does another, the reviewer catches it. It also runs on its own. You can set it up to post a pipeline coverage update to sales every Monday, or flag when a metric moves outside a range you care about. Or if youre old fashioned it can make a beautiful dashboard for you :) It connects to Postgres, MySQL, BigQuery, Snowflake, Databricks. Also Datadog, Sentry, PostHog, Coda, Notion, GitHub, and anything else via MCP. It reads your Slack history to see how things were answered before. It reads your codebase to understand how a metric is actually calculated. Charts, dashboards, PDFs, exports, whatever format you need. Most answers come back in under a minute. The benchmark We ran Datost against BIRD Interact, the hardest public text to SQL benchmark. 600 deliberately ambiguous questions across 22 messy and realistic PostgreSQL databases, published at ICLR 2026. Every question uses terms that aren’t in the schema. Claude Opus 4.6, the model Datost runs on, gets 33% on its own. Datost scored 75.2%. Same model. The gap is the semantic layer. On analytical questions, 91%. Full breakdown here: <https://datost.com/blog/bird interact Ask If your team has a Slack, Teams, or Google Chat workspace and data people keep asking about, we can get you running in a day. Book a call (https://datost.cal.com/founders/30 min) Email me or Jason: maceo@datost.com (mailto:maceo@datost.com) or jason@datost.com (mailto:jason@datost.com) Or reply here <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=iIxir5wtX44

Figma, Rippling and Uber already use our agent to create ABM campaigns and move deals forward with tailored business cases, deal rooms, case studies and more. Comment “Mutiny” on our LinkedIn (https://www.linkedin.com/posts/jalehr we raised 72m from sequoiayc and hit 8 activity 7447310976070283264 1NRa?utm source=share&utm medium=member desktop&rcm=ACoAACKHswAB8YARQchGlnITcnK7Y raTYmZGAE) or X post (https://x.com/jalehr/status/2041546131709292586?s=20) & we’ll triple your AI credits for free. Launch video: <https://youtu.be/ OfqWVrQj3s The Problem: Dependencies are the 1 killer of revenue. A decade of leading GTM teams taught me that dependencies are the biggest blocker to growth. Too many great ideas die waiting for resources: design, content, ops. We started Mutiny to let sellers and marketers move at the speed of their ideas, building experiences that wow buyers and close deals. Mutiny 1.0 started as a big success: $72M from tier 1 investors like Sequoia/YC, 8 figures in ARR, and beloved by the best GTM teams in the world. But it was built on a SaaS foundation, which puts the execution onus on humans. No matter how much we injected AI, we couldn’t unlock the real power of automation. So we killed the SaaS product and rebuilt the whole thing from scratch, this time agent first and with even bigger ambitions. The solution: Meet the new Mutiny. Mutiny is the first AI agent that gives GTM teams full autonomy to create whatever they need to hit their goals, on brand and personalized. You can overcome every challenge when prospecting and closing deals: A tailored prospecting page for a senior exec at your target account. A polished business case that sells for you when you're not in the room. A curated customer story that creates urgency. A competitor comparison that takes your rival off the table. A deal room your champion is proud to share, . One person with Mutiny can do more in an afternoon than a team in a month. The best GTM teams love the new Mutiny already. 30,000+ assets published. Users from Rippling, Snowflake & startups say: Speed: 4.5X faster to create assets Quality: 100% said it meets or exceeds their design bar Impact: 4 out of 5 reps are more likely to hit quota with Mutiny Edge: 89% say it gives them an edge in competitive deals Differentiation: Quality is what sets Mutiny apart. It’s 100% on brand. 1. On brand, every time. Mutiny extracts your colors, fonts and visual style from your website to set the guardrails, so every asset looks and sounds like you. 2. Personalized, automatically. Mutiny researches your prospect and pulls in your CRM data and call transcripts, so every asset is instantly tailored to the account. Your champion is forwarding decks & pitching you in meetings. Mutiny shows who opened your assets & what they read, so you can sell even when you're not in the room & engage new stakeholders w/ context. 4. Proven GTM best practices. Our agent has analyzed top performing GTM assets and frameworks and built templates for every deal stage. The agent applies your brand and context so you get a polished asset in minutes. Sign up for free: MutinyHQ.com (http://MutinyHQ.com) Comment “Mutiny” on our X or LinkedIn post & we’ll triple your credits for free. records.description: Humwork lets AI agents chat with human experts in real-time. When AI agents like Claude Code or OpenClaw hit a wall, Humwork's MCP server connects them to a verified domain expert in under 30 seconds. Our experts include senior engineers, lawyers, marketers, designers, and more.
records.launchTitle: Humwork - Connect AI agents with human experts in 30 seconds
records.tagline: When AI agents hit a wall, Humwork connects them to a verified expert in real-time
records.launchBodyText: Hey everyone 👋 (https://emojipedia.org/waving hand)\ \ We’re Yash (https://www.linkedin.com/in/yashvg/) and Rohan (https://www.linkedin.com/in/rohan datta 2500/), co founders of Humwork (https://humwork.ai/). TL;DR: Humwork is the real time marketplace where AI agents call verified human experts when they get stuck. Add Humwork to any MCP compatible agent, and when your coding agent, marketing agent, or research agent hits a wall, it gets connected to the right expert in under 30 seconds.\ \ <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Bqw qP jubA \ Here’s the strange but true version of what we’re building: AI agents will pay humans to chat with them. \ \ That sounds futuristic, but the need is already here. Anyone using tools like Claude Code, Lovable, or OpenClaw has seen the same failure mode. The agent gets 80% of the way there, then loops on the same bug, makes the same bad architectural guess five times, hallucinates an important legal nuance, misses the brand judgment call, or quietly produces something that looks right but is subtly wrong. If you’re not an expert yourself, you usually can’t even tell exactly where it went off the rails.\ \ That is the gap Humwork fills. \ \ When an agent gets stuck, it calls Humwork through MCP. We route it to the right verified expert in real time — a senior engineer, lawyer, marketer, designer, scientist, or other domain specialist — and the expert talks directly to the agent, not to the user. The answer gets pushed back into the agent’s context, and the work continues without the user leaving their workflow.\ \ In practice, this means a coding agent stuck in a debugging loop can escalate to a senior engineer. A legal agent unsure whether a clause will actually hold up can escalate to a lawyer. A marketing agent that produced something polished but generic can escalate to someone with real taste and positioning judgment. Instead of failing silently or asking the user to rescue it, the agent gets expert help on demand.\ \ Developers and vibe coders hit this problem every day. A problem that a strong engineer can diagnose in a few minutes can send an agent into a 45 minute death spiral. And for non technical builders, it’s even worse: the AI makes a mistake, but they don’t know enough to tell it how to recover. Their project stalls, not because the AI was useless, but because it needed one moment of human expertise.\ \ Humwork gives AI agents a way to ask for expert help. Our view is that this is where AI is going across all knowledge work. AI will do most of the execution, but humans will still sit at the edge for the hard decisions: architecture, compliance, judgment, taste, tradeoffs, and exceptions. Waymo has remote driver assistance for edge cases. We’re building the equivalent for AI agents. Humwork is live today. You can add one MCP server and give your agent an escalation path to verified experts in under a minute. 💬 Our Ask If you’re building with AI agents, try Humwork at humwork.ai (http://humwork.ai). If you’re building an AI agent product or running internal agents at your company, we’d love to talk about integrating Humwork as your human escalation layer. And if you’re an exceptional engineer, lawyer, marketer, designer, doctor, or domain expert who wants to get paid to help AI systems, we’d love to have you join the network. You can reach us at founders@humwork.ai (mailto:founders@humwork.ai) . 👬 The Team Yash and Rohan have been friends for 16 years, and both went to UC Berkeley for college.\ \ Yash (CEO) was previously and AI engineer and is a 2x founder. He’s a patent holder for graphene supercapacitor manufacturing and built his first LLM startup in 2021. He studied Data Science at UC Berkeley.\ \ Rohan (CTO) has worked as Data Scientist at Microgrid Labs and did drone imagine research at Berkeley. He’s previously built an AI voice calling platform that automated over 1 million minutes of phone calls. Rohan got his BS and MS in Civil Engineering from UC Berkeley.

records.description: Intuned is a code-first browser automation platform with an AI agent that builds and maintains your automations. Developers use Intuned to deploy scrapers, RPAs, and crawlers as production-ready TypeScript or Python projects — with stealth, CAPTCHA solving, proxies, auth, and scheduling handled out of the box. The agent builds projects from a prompt, fixes failed runs, and can self-heal automations autonomously when websites change.
records.launchTitle: Intuned - Code-first browser automation, built and maintained by AI
records.tagline: We help developers build and maintain browser automations (scrapers, RPAs, crawlers) with an AI agent that writes the code and fixes it when websites change.
records.tags: Developer Tools, Robotic Process Automation, B2B, API, AI
records.launchBodyText: Hey Everyone, I'm Faisal, co founder of Intuned. We're a code first browser automation platform with an AI agent that builds and maintains your automations: scrapers, RPAs, crawlers, and anything else that needs to control a browser at scale. <https://youtu.be/22IYRA ZqQk The problem Browser automations run a surprising amount of the internet's back office: price monitoring, job aggregators, data migrations, every RPA that exists because a vendor won't ship an API. And they're miserable to own. The scripts are fragile, selectors break constantly, and when a site changes, someone's day is gone. That's before the infrastructure: stealth, proxies, CAPTCHAs, authentication, scheduling, retries, observability. Building one automation is annoying. Running a hundred in production is a full time team. Recent AI browsers and LLM driven agents handle one off tasks well, but for repetitive tasks that run regularly, you need the speed, predictability, and cost profile that only code gives you. Nobody wants to write that code. And nobody wants to maintain it. How Intuned works Two parts, designed to work together. The platform. Write automations in TypeScript or Python, organize them into Projects, deploy to Intuned. Run them on demand via API or on a schedule. We handle stealth, CAPTCHA solving, proxies, AuthSessions for login flows, batched Jobs with retries and concurrency, and full observability (logs, browser traces, session recordings). Intuned Agent. Describe what you want. The agent explores the site, proposes a schema, writes the code, validates it against the live site, and delivers a production ready Project you can review and deploy. It also edits existing projects and diagnoses failed runs by pulling real traces and logs, the same signals a human engineer would use. Learn more about it in our docs. (https://intunedhq.com/docs/main/02 intuned agent/overview) Self healing projects. Flip a switch and the project becomes autonomous. When a site changes and a run fails, Intuned detects it, the agent diagnoses and fixes it, and the project redeploys. You configure how much autonomy it gets. learn more about it in our docs (https://intunedhq.com/docs/main/02 intuned agent/self healing projects). More demos: Build a scraper (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=Es390qwW87w) Build an RPA (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=D1Uf42OS1cs) Fix with AI (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=dPqyOy brOE) self healing projects (https://youtu.be/22IYRA ZqQk) Use cases Scrapers — pricing, job listings, public records, news, product catalogs. Anything structured you need on a schedule. RPA — form submissions, data entry, report downloads, account actions. Essential when the service you need to integrate with doesn't have an API. Migrations — moving data between services that don't talk to each other Crawlers — multi page discovery, sitemaps, content indexing at scale How we got here We've been in browser automation for over two years. We launched Intuned as a developer platform, but the 1 ask from customers was for us to just build and maintain the automations for them. We ended up running a services motion alongside the product, and over the past year we've processed 40M automation runs (20M minutes) for a limited set of customers. It became one of the most loved parts of working with us, and also the thing we most wanted to replace with product. We spent the last year trying, and nothing we built was good enough. Then we integrated the Claude Agent SDK (Claude code wrapped as SDK) directly into Intuned. Combined with the platform we'd already built, the agent now does what a human engineer on our team used to do: build projects, validate against the live site, read failed run traces, and ship fixes. Our ask Checkout our docs here (https://intunedhq.com/docs/main/00 getting started/introduction). Try it free: app.intuned.io (https://app.intuned.io) Have a use case you want to talk through? Book a demo (https://cal.com/team/intuned/get started) or email support@intunedhq.com (https://mailto:support@intunedhq.com) Share it with anyone you think will be interested. Thanks for reading 🙏 Faisal & the Intuned team

records.description: Letterbook is the AI-native alternative to Zendesk, Freshdesk, Zohodesk, and Intercom. We help companies manage and resolve customer support tickets across email, in-app feedback, and website forms. Our AI will triage your tickets, write replies, and build your knowledge base.
records.launchTitle: Letterbook - AI Customer Support Built for Startups
records.tagline: Connect your inbox, database, and Stripe. AI resolves your customer service tickets.
records.tags: Artificial Intelligence, Customer Service, Customer Support
records.launchBodyText: Hey everyone, we’re Dawson and Ethan, the founders of Letterbook. The Problem We grew our last product, Martin (an AI personal assistant), to 100,000 users. At one point, we'd get 50 customer support tickets every day, across email and help forms: "How do I use this feature?" "Can I get a refund?" "I can't log into my account." I had no choice but to hire a virtual assistant in the Philippines to reply to users and solve billing issues. I had to use Zendesk to manage tickets, which was built 20 years ago and designed for a VP of Support, not a founder. The Solution None of this made sense, so we built our own modern, AI native support platform. Letterbook integrates with your database, knowledge base, and Stripe, and our AI auto prepares resolutions to every ticket. All you have to do is review and send. Watch our launch video: <https://youtu.be/S1vNoMTcKoI?si=sFwRk9Hpu95ibeqm Results We’ve already replaced Intercom, Zendesk, Zohodesk, Helpscout, and Freshdesk for many fast growing companies in YC. One founder said: The AI drafts save us so much time, and it gets better over time without us doing anything (it learns from our past emails). When we have to make changes to the draft, the CMD+k feature is a game changer and allows us to fine tune the response without copying and pasting the entire thread to ChatGPT. Try it for free Sign up at <https://letterbook.ai ! Start resolving tickets in 3 easy steps: 1. Connect your support@yourdomain.com (mailto:support@yourdomain.com) inbox. 2. Connect your database and/or Stripe by pasting a restricted API key in Letterbook. 3. Review the first draft and send it! Unlike Zendesk/Freshdesk/Zohodesk, which takes days to set up (a friend literally hired a consultant to teach him), Letterbook takes 5 mins. And if you need help, you better believe we have top notch customer support! You can book a call anytime at letterbook.ai (http://letterbook.ai), or email me at dawson@letterbook.ai (mailto:dawson@letterbook.ai).

records.launchBodyText: Hey everyone! We're Adi and Abhi, the founders of Clearline. The Problem Customer experience at car dealerships is broken. Not because the teams don't care, but because the systems were never built for how customers actually behave today. Here's the reality: 23% of dealership calls go unanswered. 85% of those callers never call back. That's revenue walking out the door every single day. Most lead follow up dies by day 5. Dealerships spend thousands driving traffic and generating leads, then let them rot in a CRM nobody checks. Sales uses one system. Service uses another. Nobody has the full picture. Reps waste time on manual logging, managers guess at what's happening, and customers repeat themselves every time they call. Dealerships have tried to solve this with more tools, more staff, more process. It doesn't work. The stack is fragmented and nothing talks to each other. The Solution Clearline is an AI native operating system for dealerships. Three products, one platform: Inbound AI answers every sales and service call instantly. No voicemail, no hold music, no transfers. It qualifies intent, routes to the right department, and books appointments in real time. 24/7, including nights and weekends. Outbound Automated follow up via calls, texts, and email. Clearline responds to new inquiries within seconds and runs disciplined cadences from day 1 through day 90. No lead gets forgotten. CRM One unified record across sales, service, and BDC. Full call transcripts, automatic logging, next steps triggered without anyone typing a note. Managers get complete visibility with real data. Results Dealerships see this in their first 30 days: $60K+ revenue recovered from missed calls and dead leads 240% more leads engaged 4+ tools consolidated into one platform "Recovered $78K in service and sales revenue from 400 missed calls per month. "Show rate on outbound appointments went from 38% to 64% in the first month. You're Always in Control This isn't about replacing dealership staff. Clearline augments your team: AI escalates to a human anytime a situation needs real judgment It only operates within rules you define (hours, routing, boundaries) It only uses dealership approved responses for pricing and policies Every call, message, and booking is logged and reviewable Try Clearline If you run a dealership or know someone who does, we'd love to chat. Book a demo (https://useclearline.com/?utm source=yc&utm medium=launch&utm campaign=launch yc) Reach us directly at founders@useclearline.com (mailto:founders@useclearline.com) or check us out at Clearline (https://useclearline.com/?utm source=yc&utm medium=launch&utm campaign=launch yc)

records.launchBodyText: tl;dr: our real time, generative models turn AI into lifelike video calls. Deploy in minutes with a few lines of embed code. Photorealism, emotion, and scale for just $0.06 per minute. We’re @Kaahan Radia (https://bookface.ycombinator.com/user/569532) and @Parth Radia (https://bookface.ycombinator.com/user/3206626) from Keyframe Labs. Our interactive video models bring your AI agents and applications to life. <https://youtu.be/cIEP8OwTD4M You can try a video call for yourself here: <https://keyframelabs.com . To get a sense for the interactivity and expressive range, try asking for a sad Shakespearean monologue. Or for a “funny” dad joke. But why video agents? Face to face interaction triggers responses that text and voice simply can’t. Video calls close more deals, language learning with video tutors outperforms audio only, and human presence drives outcomes in high stakes interactions (e.g., telehealth, tax prep). Now that voice has emerged as a primary conversational interface across industries, video is the next leap. Developers and enterprises already use Keyframe to easily deploy photoreal, conversational humans to a wide variety of use cases, including: AI roleplay, for corporate training / learning and development AI recruiting, for interview practice and automated screenings Telehealth High touch customer support AI sales and customer success reps Always on digital twins of influencers, to scale engagement and hyper personalize interactions How it works Super simple. 1. Connect your agent or LLM, or choose from our supported providers 2. Select a persona from our stock library 3. Drop the embed code into your application Need a custom persona or more control? Custom personas are available on enterprise plans, and our SDKs let you roll your own implementation. Why Keyframe Our research goals from day one have been: Make meaningful progress towards beating the uncanny valley Run at real time, with world scale efficiency The result: Industry leading realism and concurrency limits At a fraction of the cost of competitors Get up and running Sign up free (https://platform.keyframelabs.com/) (no credit card required) and build something cool Have a use case? records.description: Riveter fully automates web data extraction, end to end. With a single API, agents can search, scrape, and structure data from the web. Customers like Snapchat and OpenAI use us to enrich datasets using Riveter's AI search agents that combine data from the web with third party or internal data. Their AI agents, custom scrapers, and integrations pack the power of browser use and automation for search, at a cost that allows for scale. You can even create a custom data API for repeated searches live in your app or internal databases. Perplexity and ChatGPT are great for single questions, but they can’t handle large amounts of data at once. And they’re working with a single agent and context window instead of one in every cell. And Riveter’s web browsing can go further—even reading PDFs and images that may contain answers. Most teams still spend hours (or hire UpWorkers/contractors) to manually copy product specs, check competitor pricing, pull custom KYB/KYC data to assess customer risk, or scrape filings. Or they spend months building web scrapers, and when that still doesn't work, they come to Riveter. Our founding team (Cody Watters - CTO, Abby Grills, CEO) brings deep product and engineering expertise from to YC-backed companies Gusto, Retool, and Middesk.

records.launchBodyText: Hey everyone! 👋 We're Abby and Cody, co founders of Riveter (https://riveterhq.com/) (F24). We both did 5+ years at Gusto, followed by Middesk and Retool, and watched the same painful problem play out across eng, data, ops, and growth teams: the information you need exists online, but getting it into a structured, usable dataset is brutally hard. TL;DR: Write a prompt. Riveter builds the list and enriches it scraped live from the web. No stale databases, no stitching tools together. Teams at OpenAI, Snap, and Fermat are already using Riveter to build datasets they couldn't justify building manually. Ask: We'd love to show you what Riveter can do for your use case. Book 20 minutes with us (https://riveterhq.com/demo) and we'll give you 1,000 free credits to explore the platform. <https://youtu.be/KH0IELrOtLs ❌ The Problem Almost any list you could ever need can be derived from the web. Every law firm in California. Every vet practice by state. Every competitor's pricing page. The information is out there. The problem is getting it out. Current options all hit the same wall: Data vendors sell you records from a static index outdated the moment you buy them, and missing anything outside their database Clay and Parallel pull from the same stale indexes. When we asked both to return all YC Winter 2026 companies, Parallel returned an incomplete dataset at a way higher cost. Clay returned "Y Combinator" as a company, along with results from other batches. ChatGPT and Claude can reason about the web but can't handle the scale you can't ask an LLM to return thousands of enriched records reliably The result: teams buy incomplete data, spend weeks building it manually, or go without. ✅ Our Solution Riveter agents navigate the web the way a researcher would running searches, visiting pages, reading results and return a fully structured, enriched dataset. Write one prompt. Get thousands of rows. \ Describe what you want: "Return every dental practice in San Francisco, the dentists at each practice, and their contact details" 1. Riveter agents search the web, visit pages, and compile the list live, not from a cached index 2. Custom enrichments get layered on: lead scores, pricing data, contact info, whatever your workflow needs 3. The result lands as a structured dataset, ready to use or pipe into your existing tools No code required. Use it in app, via API, or through our MCP server. 🔍 Use Cases Sales and lead generation: Find hyper specific lists no vendor has pre built. Every locksmith in the US. Every independent law firm in California. Every vet practice by state. Add enrichments in Riveter to qualify, score, and route them into your outreach. Product data: School logos, all attorneys at every AmLaw 100 firm, every menu item at every restaurant chain in a market. If it's on the web, Riveter can build it. Market intelligence: Track competitors and their pricing on a daily or weekly cadence. Especially common for e commerce teams who need to move fast when something shifts. 🙏 Our Ask Book 20 minutes with us (https://riveterhq.com/demo) tell us what you're building and we'll set up your first dataset together, plus 1,000 free credits on us Try it yourself at riveterhq.com (http://riveterhq.com) free to get started in app (https://auth.riveterhq.com/sign up), via API (https://docs.riveterhq.com/), or MCP. (https://docs.riveterhq.com/ description/mcp server) Know a team paying too much for stale data or drowning in manual research? Send this their way. 👋 The Team Abby Grills (CEO) Previously at Gusto and Middesk across product and data workflows. Riveter is the tool she kept wishing existed. Cody Watters (CTO) Previously at Gusto and Retool. Watched too much engineering time disappear into one off data pipelines that should have been solved at the platform level. We built Riveter because we kept hitting the same wall: the data we needed was obviously on the web, and there was no good way to get it out at scale.

records.launchBodyText: Hey YC! 👋 We're Upeka (https://x.com/upekabee) and Dilan (https://x.com/dilan), co founders of Diana (W24) (https://x.com/getdiana). Every OpenClaw today is an intern with root access and no oversight. ☠️🏴‍☠️ So we built the first one that has a boss. 🦞 Diana is a business ready OpenClaw with a Governor monitoring every action that can shut it down mid task, before damage is done. <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=sdka6JW9QK0 TL;DR: 🛡 Diana is a business ready OpenClaw with a Governor monitoring every action ⚡ Connect 3,000+ tools via API, or log into any app via Browser Logins 🔒 Per user credential isolation. Every employee gets their own sandboxed agent 🏗 Built by engineers from Google, Gusto, MIT, CMU, and Yale The Governor Here's the thing about OpenClaw: it talks directly to your tools. Slack, Notion, your APIs. No filter. No oversight. If it hallucinates or gets prompt injected, it acts on your systems before anyone can stop it. Diana never talks to Slack, Notion, or your APIs directly. Every action routes through the Governor first. It checks intent, filters output, and watches for drift in real time. If something is off, the Governor kills the action before it reaches your systems. Not after. Before. The architecture: → Slack ingress hits the Governor (trust boundary) → Governor routes to isolated agent sandboxes → Agent has zero DB access, zero secrets → Every LLM and tool call goes back through the Governor → Governor filters output before it reaches Slack 3,000+ Integrations + Browser Logins Diana connects to Slack, Google Workspace, HubSpot, Stripe, Notion, Linear, GitHub, and thousands more via API. And if there is no API? Diana logs in with a browser, just like you would. HRIS portals, benefits platforms, 401k providers, state registration websites. If you can click it, Diana can work it. All through the Governor. Give Your Employee Their Own Employee Don't replace your team. Give them superpowers. Diana is simple to install and use: Install to Slack → connect your tools → just ask : → "Show me our Hubspot pipeline by stage and expected close date" → "Build me a revenue dashboard in Google Sheets for our March Investor Update. Send me a revenue dashboard every month." → "Review these 5 invoices against the tracking spreadsheet let me know if there are any discrepancies" → "Every Monday at 9am, send me a summary of the general Slack channel from last week" → "Onboard Sarah Chen as Senior PM into HRIS Payroll. Here’s the offer letter." Diana works across finance, operations, sales, product, engineering, marketing, and HR. Built for every employee on earth. Hire humans, and help them to 10x. The Backstory Before Diana, we built DianaHR, a fractional HR service for SMBs. Running that business, we saw firsthand how much time operations teams waste on repetitive tasks: onboarding new hires into HRIS systems, chasing unsigned documents, answering the same benefits questions, filing state registrations. When OpenClaw exploded, we saw the opportunity: an AI agent that could actually do this work. But the security gaps were a dealbreaker for business use. So we built a Governor on top, added per user credential isolation, and created Browser Logins so Diana could work with tools that don't have APIs. Backed by Y Combinator (W24), General Catalyst, and SNR Ventures. Built by a team from Google, Gusto, MIT, Carnegie Mellon, and Amazon. And this is just the start. Our goal is an AI employee so trusted you can hand it an entire business function and let it run. Our Asks Try Diana for free. If you want an OpenClaw agent AND peace of mind: getdiana.com (http://getdiana.com) For YC founders we are giving one month free of our assistant tier. Ping me on Bookface or email for a code! Feedback. If you try Diana and something breaks or feels off, tell us. DM me on Slack, email upeka@getdiana.com (mailto:upeka@getdiana.com), or find me on X at @upekabee Let's go. 💚

records.launchTitle: ClawTrace — Make Your OpenClaw Agent Better, Cheaper, and Faster
records.tagline: The observability platform for OpenClaw agents: execution traces, cost analysis, and Tracy, your OpenClaw's doctor agent.
records.tags: SaaS, Infrastructure, AI, Databases
records.launchBodyText: 🚀 TL;DR ClawTrace (https://clawtrace.ai?utm=yc) is the observability platform for OpenClaw agents. It captures every trajectory your OpenClaw agent runs — every LLM call, tool use, and sub agent delegation — and gives you three views to understand it: an execution path, a call graph, and a timeline. When you need answers fast, ask Tracy, your built in AI analyst, in plain English. Watch our demo here (https://youtu.be/ moG7vCkawQ?si=ivl7Utv3h7ynv5rb): — Hello, world! I'm Richard, and today I'm excited to introduce ClawTrace — the observability platform for OpenClaw agents. AI agents are going to production. But right now, when something goes wrong, you're flying blind. ❌ The Problem Deploying an OpenClaw agent is easy. Understanding what it actually did is not. When a run costs $104.70 instead of the usual $1.5, you have no idea why. When an agent fails silently, you don't know which step broke or what input caused it. When a trajectory takes 3 minutes instead of 30 seconds, you can't tell where the time went. You end up reading raw JSON logs, manually counting token estimates, and guessing at execution paths. This doesn't scale. Existing options make this worse, not better: General distributed tracing tools (Jaeger, Zipkin, Datadog) weren't built for LLM concepts — they have no understanding of token counts, model costs, prompt/completion pairs, or sub agent hierarchies. Framework specific tools like LangSmith only work within their own ecosystem. If you're on OpenClaw, you're on your own. Point solutions cover one slice of the problem — just logs, just cost tracking, just evals — and leave you to stitch everything together yourself. Every tool dumps data into a dashboard and expects you to interpret it. There's no analyst. You still have to do all the thinking. ⭐️ Our Solution We built ClawTrace to give you complete visibility into every OpenClaw agent run — automatically, with zero code changes to your agent. One plugin. Three commands. Every trajectory captured. bash openclaw plugins install @epsilla/clawtrace openclaw clawtrace setup openclaw gateway restart That's it. From that point on, every agent run streams to ClawTrace automatically. With ClawTrace, you get: Execution Path — a collapsible tree of every step your agent took: LLM calls, tool executions, sub agent delegations, with full input and output payloads at every node. Metadata badges show model name, duration, token counts, and estimated cost inline. Call Graph — an interactive force directed diagram showing the relationships between every agent, model, and tool in a run. Understand the shape of a complex multi agent workflow at a glance. Timeline — a Gantt chart of every span in the trajectory, positioned by start time and sized by duration. Bottlenecks, parallelism, and idle gaps become immediately visible. Tracy, your OpenClaw’s Doctor — every other observability tool gives you data and expects you to figure it out yourself. Tracy is different. Ask her a question in plain English — "where is the bottleneck in this trace?" — and she queries your trajectory graph live, generates a visualization, and delivers specific recommendations: which step to fix, why, and how. Tracy is powered by a Claude managed agent and has real time Cypher access to your full trajectory graph via PuppyGraph graph lake house architecture. Cost Estimation — per span cost calculation covering 80+ models across OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, DeepSeek, Mistral, Alibaba Qwen, Zhipu GLM, Moonshot, and more. Cache aware pricing (fresh input vs. cached input vs. output tokens) for accurate estimates. Consumption based billing — pay for what you use with credits. No seat fees, no per user pricing. Start with 100 free credits. 🔦 The Ask AI agents are moving from demos to production. ClawTrace is the observability layer that makes that transition safe, debuggable, and cost efficient. 🚀 Sign up for free at clawtrace.ai (http://clawtrace.ai) — 100 credits included, your first trajectory shows up in minutes. ⭐ Star us on GitHub — ClawTrace is fully open source under Apache 2.0: github.com/epsilla cloud/clawtrace (https://github.com/epsilla cloud/clawtrace) 💬 Tell us what you're building — we'd love to hear about your OpenClaw setup and what observability problems matter most to you. Schedule a call with me (https://epsilla ai.sg.larksuite.com/scheduler/dc6a62e558c3a1cc).

records.launchBodyText: Runtime (runtm.com (http://runtm.com)) lets your whole team ship safely with any coding agent. It gives your team the guardrails, sandboxes, context, and visibility to go from idea to PR or deployment, with any model, on any infra.\ \ Our coding agents set your repo environments automatically: You can use Runtime through our web interface: Or anyone on your team can trigger it from Slack, Linear, and many other connectors: Launch video:\ <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=m3H8yUbdIHY The problem After my last company was acquired, I led our coding agents rollout and personally shipped 4+ full stack products in the first 3 months. I then started enabling the rest of the team to do the same. But we hit a lot of friction fast: we needed to create working environments, we didn’t have any control or context to ensure agents contributed correctly, and nobody had visibility of what was being shipped. And because we'd gone deep on one provider's tooling, when their reliability dipped, the whole team slowed down. Everyone rolling out coding agents across their team hits the same walls: No environment setup: No easy way to give engineering and product teams production like environments where agents can safely run. No guardrails: No access controls, system instructions, or secrets management for agents. No visibility: No way to see what agents are doing, what they're changing, or where things break. Vendor lock in: Go all in on one model provider's agent infra and your whole team is exposed when reliability, pricing, or model quality shifts. Without these, you end up either moving slower than your competition or causing major downtimes on your product. What we built Runtime sits between your team and the coding agents they use. It works with Claude Code, Codex, OpenCode, Gemini, Copilot, and others, and can be called programmatically or used through Slack, Linear, Jira, Asana, Teams, CLI, and more. \ Here's what that looks like in practice: Environment templates: Import any repo and set up a replica of your environments once. Our agents handle the setup for you, even for monorepos and microservices. Sandboxed environments: Anyone on the team can spin up a sandbox in milliseconds with a live preview of your product, make changes with their preferred coding agent, and open a PR. Configurable guardrails: System instructions, skills, allowlists, encrypted secrets, and role based access control for both humans and agents. Observability: See exactly what every human and agent is doing across every interaction, down to the tool call and chain of thought. One click deploys / PRs: Anyone can deploy from their sandbox. Coding agents auto fix build errors and verify the deployment is healthy before it goes live. You control what gets deployed and can roll back or destroy deployments at any time. Runtime can be fully self hosted.\ \ Check our product demo: <https://youtu.be/Gqdzfo7a8IM \ \ Asks Use Runtime at your company. Sign up at app.runtm.com (http://app.runtm.com). If you're a CTO, head of platform, or eng / product leader rolling out coding agents, our team will personally set up your environments and help you set up best practices to your team is shipping within the first days. Book a demo at <https://cal.com/gustavo trigos q80bop/runtime demo meeting Upvote this and connect with me to follow the journey: <https://www.linkedin.com/in/gustavoatrigos/ <https://x.com/gustrigos

records.description: Arga provides on-demand staging per PR, so anyone (human or agent) can test any change. Deploy a change into our sandbox and it runs against twins of any SaaS (Stripe, Google Drive, Slack, etc.), with no config required. These twins implement the same APIs, are fully compatible with any SDK, surface edge cases mocks or assertions miss, and you never hit rate limits. For AI agents, run red-teams with Arga: test tool calls, behaviours, and failure modes before anything reaches production.
records.launchTitle: Arga Labs: on-demand, production-like staging for every PR
records.tagline: Don’t be a 1x tester when you’re a 100x engineer
records.tags: Developer Tools, SaaS, Infrastructure
records.launchBodyText: tl;dr: become a 100x tester with Arga by giving your agents staging environments scoped to a change. We spin up twins of your integrations (Slack, Stripe, etc.) and dependencies (DB, Redis), and deploy only the services that were changed. Everything else is routed to prod. Launch Video: <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=OzEk5pYTvak The problem: You’re a 1x tester You don’t have staging :( You have staging, but it’s different from prod, and things still break after testing You mock integrations, which can be inaccurate You use real accounts to test integrations, but you hit rate limits, can’t clear states between tests, and can’t test at scale You have staging, but your agents can’t automatically test changes so you’re stuck at being a 1x tester despite being a 100x engineer You have staging, but it’s difficult to merge concurrent changes without breaking something (for larger teams!) Our Solution We spin up a temporary staging of your app every time you open a PR. Only the services you change are redeployed; all other services are routed to prod. Your dependencies are deployed as in memory sidecars, so nothing corrupts prod data. For example, you can configure the tests to read from the prod database and perform all write commands to the sidecar; you can also configure everything to be routed to the sidecar. We also spin up twins of services your app uses: Stripe, Slack, GSuite, Hubspot, and more, so you don’t hit the real API. This lets you test more thoroughly: you can catch weird edge cases, simulate failure modes, and run tests in parallel without worrying about rate limits. All twins are fully compatible with any SDK, expose the same API endpoints, and support webhook events. You can seed any twin in natural language, or select from our pre set scenarios. Demo of Github, Slack, Stripe twins on the Arga app: <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=3ZPrIdGvPoo You or your agent can prompt Arga via web, API, CLI, or MCP to automatically generate tests for your PR, run them, and stream the results and logs back . This allows them to know what was tested, what broke, and which calls/functions caused the failure. They can use this information to go back and fix the code, so you’ll have a fully tested and passing PR before you look at it. Validation report: Claude Code using arga CLI to read logs & test results: Our ask Try us out! We’re live at www.argalabs.com (http://www.argalabs.com) and you can get access to limited features for free. \ If you like our stuff, book a demo here (<https://cal.com/phillip li/30min ).\ If you know any teams that struggle with testing and use lots of external integrations, send them our way. The Team Akira and Phillip met each other in first year calculus at UBC. At the time, Akira was 14 and Phillip was studying neuroscience to pursue research in human biology. Within a year of pivoting to CS, Phillip built a dev tool at Amazon that saved 10 weeks / year of engineering hours as an intern. While interning at Stripe, Akira realized that a high fidelity “staging” was the key to unlocking dev productivity and was surprised that most companies don’t have proper staging that actually mirrored prod. Now they’re solving that problem.

records.description: Silmaril is self-healing prompt injection defense for AI native applications and agents. It understands application context to block 2x as many threats as current SOTA defenses with 10x lower latency. Customers plug us into agentic frameworks like LangGraph with 5 lines of code. Silmaril has stopped $28M of damages for customers.
records.launchTitle: Silmaril: Self-healing Prompt Injection Defense
records.tagline: Block 2x more attacks with 10x lower latency.
records.tags: Security
records.launchBodyText: TL;DR Silmaril is the first self healing prompt injection defense for AI native applications and agents. It understands application context to block 2x as many threats as current SOTA defenses with 10x lower latency . Customers plug us into frameworks like LangChain with 5 lines of code . We’re working with the top productivity, analytics, and legal companies in the world and would love to protect you as well! Our Launch Video: <https://youtu.be/95VAZSvIsLA Ask : If your AI systems ingest external data or you’re concerned about prompt injection, set up a 15 minute chat (https://cal.com/silmaril/15min). If you know security or AI leaders (Director+) who could benefit from stopping prompt injections please reach out at aum@silmaril.dev (mailto:aum@silmaril.dev). Problem Attackers are using AI to create and experiment with more sophisticated techniques at scale, making them 4.5x more likely to succeed . The AI security tools enterprises rely on today were not built for this. Existing guardrails treat malicious inputs as the unit of security. They pattern match against known bad prompts and require constant manual policy tuning. Against real world contextual and emerging threats, leading guardrails blocked only 61% of attacks . That's barely better a coin toss. This leaves security teams stuck in an endless loop : triaging alerts, tuning policies, and chasing attackers who move faster than they can respond. How it works Most AI security tools ask "does this input look malicious?" We ask "does this execution lead to a harmful outcome?" That distinction is what makes Silmaril work against attacks that guardrails completely miss. The system has three layers: 1. Agents hunt for attacks and generate threat intelligence. We reverse engineer your application and deploy autonomous agents that continuously probe it. These agents find and verify attack chains that combine individual vulnerabilities for a larger impact such as data exfiltration and privilege escalation. They surface novel threats tied to your application logic, far more useful in simulating AI enabled attackers than historical prompt injection lists. 2. A classifier model that blocks attackers in real time. The firewall integrates directly with your orchestration framework via our SDK, letting you control exactly when it runs. In LangGraph, for example, you can add it as a callback handler that fires before/after tool calls and model inference. Because Silmaril reasons over application snapshots rather than input patterns, it catches emerging, contextual, and multi turn attacks that single input classifiers miss entirely. Latency overhead is 20ms. 3. A self healing retraining loop. Verified exploits from threat hunting and production traffic feeds back into the model continuously. The firewall retrains and deploys updated weights automatically within an hour. No manual policy tuning required. Against a dataset of real world contextual and emerging threats, Silmaril blocked 96% of attacks . Leading guardrails blocked 61%. Why us Eduardo and I spent 10+ years building security and AI. I built security frameworks protecting AWS’s AI infrastructure. Eduardo developed hardened models and agents for Amazon's homepage. We drove $2.2B in savings and new revenue . After leaving, we became whitehat hackers and hacked OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and Microsoft 15 times in two weeks . We saw firsthand how far ahead attackers already are. That's why we built Silmaril.

records.launchBodyText: TL;DR: Create your dream GTM motion at the speed of thought. Describe your ideal buyer or buying trigger in a single sentence. Avina finds matching companies 24/7, enriches them with verified contacts, writes personalized sequences tied to the exact signal, and syncs leads wherever your team works. Signal to AI outbound, fully automated. Starting at $149/mo. 👋 Hey friends, we're Vivek and Mike (YC S22). We previously built and sold Bowtie to Mindbody, and now we're building Avina to fix outbound. <https://youtu.be/rdUnYD9Z6Ac The problem Generic outbound is dying, but doing smart outbound is still painfully hard. The pitches that get replies are the ones that arrive right when something happening at the prospect's company makes your product feel relevant. The hard part has always been finding those moments at scale and acting on them fast. Our solution Avina finds the companies that actually need your product right now, qualifies them, and automatically engages them with personalized AI outreach. All synced with your existing sales stack. How it works Describe your ideal buyer or buying trigger in a single sentence: Selling usage based billing? "PLG companies that just launched an API in the last 3 months" Selling compliance automation? "Companies awarded a prime DoD contract in the last 30 days" Selling cybersecurity? "Healthcare companies that experienced a data breach in the last 6 months" Selling voice AI? "Mid market home services companies with recent negative reviews mentioning 'long wait times' or 'understaffed'" It works for first party intent too. Website visitor de anonymization is built in (RB2B, Vector, and more), so you can act on inbound signals like "prospects that visited our pricing page for more than 2 min.” Avina takes it from there. records.launchBodyText: Hey everyone! We're Rachel and Kimi, the founders of CambioML. We are excited to introduce ERPNow . 🚀 TL;DR ERPNow is an AI analyst that sits on top of your existing ERP (SAP, Oracle, NetSuite) to turn tons of operational data into real time operational insights and future revenue. It doesn’t just show you charts; it detects risks, explains root causes in plain language, and recommends exactly what to do next. ❌ The Problem Supply chain teams are drowning in data but starving for insights. Traditional ERP systems are powerful but static—reports are slow, forecasts break the moment reality changes, and analysts spend weeks answering "why did this happen?" instead of "what should we do next?" By the time an exception is surfaced in a dashboard, the damage (stockouts or delays) is often already done. 🛠️ Our Solution ERPNow acts as an intelligent layer over your ERP, WMS, and spreadsheets. It provides: Real time Risk Detection: Monitors your supply chain 24/7 to surface anomalies (like a supplier delay or demand spike) the moment they appear. Plain Language Root Cause Analysis: Ask "Why is this SKU at risk?" and get a cited answer explaining that a specific supplier lead time increased by 40%, rather than just seeing a red bar on a chart. Actionable Recommendations: It suggests specific fixes—like transferring inventory between warehouses or expediting a PO—to prevent issues before they escalate. Zero Workflow Disruption: No ERP replacement required. It’s read only by default and deploys in weeks, not quarters. You can find a demo here: <https://youtu.be/fnV2vXpkq4k \ \ 🧑‍🏫 Meet Our Team Rachel Hu, CEO: Former ML scientist at AWS AI, UC Berkeley alum, Forbes 30 Under 30. Kimi Kong, CTO: Former research engineer at Google DeepMind, Microsoft & AWS; Stanford alum. 📖 Our Story After building Energent.ai (http://Energent.ai) to handle general data analysis, we realized that supply chain and operations teams faced the most "messy" data challenges. ERP systems are often black boxes. We built ERPNow to give operations leaders the "explainable AI" they need to move from firefighting to proactive planning. 🎯 Our Ask 👉 Try it out: If you’re managing complex supply chains or struggling with ERP reporting, visit www.erpnow.io (http://www.erpnow.io). 👉 Get a Demo: Reach out directly to admin@cambioml.com (mailto:admin@cambioml.com) to see how we can sit on top of your specific ERP setup. 👉 Spread the word: If you know any Operations or Supply Chain VPs, we’d love an introduction! Cheers, The ERPNow Team ❤️

records.description: ## What is Yuma AI? Yuma is automating customer support by providing an AI Support Agent orchestration platform. Yuma provides autonomous AI agents dedicated to customer service and e-commerce. We integrate directly with help desk software (Zendesk, Kustomer, Gorgias, etc.) and focus on helping large Shopify brands deal with their high volume of support requests. Top merchants using Yuma can automate up to 80% of their support tickets fully autonomously. ## Why do Shopify merchants need Yuma? There are thousands of large Shopify merchants around the world generating $10M+ a year. Most of them have taken over some niches and are great at what they do: selling their products. But they all have one thing in common: they all hate customer support. It's a burden for them and a huge source of cost, as they receive hundreds of requests per day. Some of them have dozens of support agents on staff, usually offshore, and the overall quality is still often pretty poor while staff turnover remains high. Those merchants have been promised automation for years but have always been disappointed by the outcome. As customers, we have all had to live through a terrible support automation experience before, so this is no secret, and everyone knows that the current state of the technology is quite bad. ## Yuma is solving this in a few ways: Yuma isn't just another RAG chatbot. Our platform provides autonomous AI agents dedicated to support and ecommerce. These agents are powered by knowledge, follow processes, and are managed by our in-house AI orchestration technology. This ensures safety, control, and quality at every step. We believe that to truly automate customer support, we must develop a platform that mimics the actions of a human support agent. That's exactly what we've been doing. Currently, our top merchants are automating 60% of their support tickets through Yuma. Yuma can fetch information from external services and take actions in other apps as needed to resolve each ticket. We are laser-focused on building the best AI support agent platform and aim to achieve 80% true automation in the next few months. ## Fun Fact Yuma got started by accident. This is my third YC startup after Socialcam (W12) and Triplebyte (S15). I released Yuma as a prototype for fun in mid-December 2022 and was overwhelmed with demo requests. That's when I knew I was onto something and had to turn this into a real company, once again.

records.launchBodyText: 👋 We are Yuma (YC W23) , the AI agent layer for commerce CX. We started building on OpenAI's Davinci model in late 2022 and have processed millions of customer conversations for 100+ commerce brands since. Today we are launching Ask Yuma . Think Claude Code, but for your entire CX operation. A conversational AI built into every page of the Yuma dashboard. Demo video: <https://youtu.be/qqc3XGCEHAc?si=8X4Lx4DatRe1fJMX Ask Yuma knows your entire support operation: tickets, automations, knowledge base, metrics, integrations, brand voice. You tell it what you want in plain English. It does the work. Why we built it Since 2023, Yuma has automated millions of customer interactions for commerce brands. Our best merchants automate 93% of their customer conversations. The automation works. But configuring it was still painful. Merchants had SOPs in Google Docs they couldn't turn into live automations without our help. They wanted to investigate why a ticket was handled wrong but had to file a request and wait. They knew there were more tickets to automate but finding them meant hours of manual digging. The dependency on our team was the bottleneck. So we built Ask Yuma: one conversational interface that puts all of that in the merchant's hands, with our team still there when they need us. What it does Build automations from documents: Upload any SOP or process doc. Ask Yuma reads it, asks clarifying questions, generates a visual flowchart, and produces a complete automation ready to go live. What used to take days takes 1 conversation. Find automation opportunities: Ask Yuma analyzes your escalated tickets, ranks recommendations by impact, and links to real examples so you can verify before acting. Diagnose issues: Ask "why was this ticket handled wrong?" Ask Yuma traces the root cause through your configuration and tells you what to change. Generate reports: Ask for a report of every product defect reported this week. Ask Yuma searches thousands of tickets, pulls the images, and generates a PDF you can share with your exec team or manufacturer. Monitor performance: Track automation rate, CSAT trends, unusual spikes, and compare time periods in plain English. No dashboards to build. It doesn't stop there. Ask Yuma finds what's broken, proposes a plan, gets your approval, implements the fix, tests it in a sandbox, and verifies it worked. In just 1 week of internal release, Ask Yuma has seen 60% adoption among existing merchants. Looking for Commerce brands running support automation that want to move faster without the manual work. We will connect Ask Yuma and help you find automation opportunities on day one. Reach out to Guillaume (founder) at g social ai launch@yuma.ai (mailto:g social ai launch@yuma.ai). What else does Yuma do Yuma is a full AI agent infrastructure for commerce brands. Beyond Ask Yuma, we offer autonomous support agents, a sales AI widget for product pages, social media comment automation, deterministic workflow builder, and deep ticket analytics. All connected, all powered by the same orchestration layer. What should we build next? We are working on automated weekly process recommendations, scheduled reports, and MCP integration so you can use Ask Yuma directly inside Claude and other AI tools. Tell us what workflow you wish you could ask your support data about. We build based on what brands need most.

records.launchBodyText: Most AI contract tools are built to support a single lawyer working within Word. Gerri is built for everyone with a stake in getting a deal signed. (https://www.getgerri.ai/) 2 minute intro: youtube.com/watch?v=QL4nIETkZnY (https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=QL4nIETkZnY) 3 minute demo: loom.com/share/ac7906243ce340159797af888488cb6b (https://www.loom.com/share/ac7906243ce340159797af888488cb6b) Background: More than 10,000 companies have used Common Paper to close deals over the past few years. While the standards have helped, we keep hearing how painful the redline process is. The root cause is that contract negotiation is a team sport, but most tools are built for one player. Most teams end up in one of two camps: Every redline goes to the attorney, which is expensive and slows deals down Redlines get triaged first, usually by someone in sales or ops, and contracts are one of 10 things they have on their plate What's missing is a system where AI does the first read, loops in the right people when needed, and pulls all of their feedback into a new draft that can be sent back to the other side. That's Gerri. How it works: Contracts get into Gerri three ways: upload, forwarded email, or direct inbox scanning. From there: Gerri reads every redline against your playbook and automatically accepts, rejects, or counterproposes anything it can handle Anything outside the playbook gets routed to the right person. That might be legal for indemnity questions, finance for payment terms, VP of Sales for commercial terms, etc. Every AI decision cites the specific playbook rule behind it; every human override is logged Gerri edits the Word doc directly and can add comments in your name, explaining the reasoning to the other side Gerri typically reviews contracts before you even know they've arrived. You'll open your inbox, see a contract, and find it's already been handled. What's new in 2.0: 10X faster processing engine. 90% of contracts reviewed in under 3 minutes. Any contract type: your paper, their paper, standard or custom agreements Personality Settings: set custom instructions that guide Gerri's behavior across all contracts, without editing individual playbook rules Template Improvement: Gerri analyzes your negotiation history and suggests changes to your outgoing templates that proactively reduce future redlines. The more you use it, the smarter your paper gets. Optional attorney review add on: flat fee, 4 hour turnaround guarantee, for when you need a human in the loop Available as a standalone product at getgerri.ai (http://getgerri.ai) No other Common Paper subscription required Try it free at getgerri.ai (http://getgerri.ai)

records.description: AI chatbots can answer questions, but they can't do your work — they aren't connected to your tools, can't take actions, and stop running when you close the tab. Meanwhile, knowledge workers are still spending most of their day switching between dozens of SaaS apps, manually stitching together workflows that should be automated. Tasklet is the AI agent that actually does the work. It connects to every tool you use — via thousands of built-in integrations, any HTTP API, or MCP servers — and takes real actions on your behalf: pulling reports, updating CRMs, triaging emails, processing data, and more. When it needs a UI, it generates one on the fly. When it needs to crunch numbers or do bulk operations, it writes and runs code in a cloud sandbox. When it needs to interact with a website that doesn't have an API, it uses a real browser. And because it runs in the cloud 24/7, you can set up triggers to have it take ongoing responsibility for workflows — not just do something once, but handle it going forward. The product is deliberately simple: a chat interface where you talk to an extremely capable agent. But underneath that simplicity is a cloud-native agent runtime, best-in-class context engineering, a universal connections layer, and multi-model support that keeps us on the frontier as AI improves. We think legacy SaaS is being unbundled by agents, and Tasklet is the operating system that ties it all together.

records.description: Driver is the context layer that translates your codebase into shared, structured understanding. By computing context ahead of time, Driver delivers symbol‑complete documentation, architecture maps, and history guides so teams and agents can reason across an entire codebase without guesswork.
records.launchTitle: Driver:  Context for Codebases
records.tagline: Translate your codebase into shared, structured understanding (for humans and AI.)
records.launchBodyText: The gist: Driver (https://www.driverai.com/) uses a compiler inspired system to pre compute exhaustive understanding of your codebases and serve it to AI coding agents via MCP. With Driver's content, Cursor, Claude Code, and Copilot never get lost or go down the wrong rabbit holes enabling you to build fearlessly at scale. "We spend a ton of time on the planning and research phase of our agentic SDLC — Driver is where the real value is for us. It's helping our engineers dramatically on story tasking alone, and it's literally going viral inside our company. I have to tell my engineers to hold on while we get everything set up." — CTO, Leading E commerce Fulfillment Platform Hi everyone, we're Adam (https://www.linkedin.com/in/adamktilton/) and Daniel (https://linkedin.com/in/daniel hensley). We launched Driver on Bookface two years ago as a documentation tool. A lot has changed. What actually happened in two years We started by building a system to produces accurate, symbol complete documentation. It became the source of truth for our customers across their engineering orgs. This evolved quickly into AI agents reading the docs. Customers began checking our docs directly into their repos as markdown files making them available to Cursor and Claude Code. The shift to context for agents unlocked value at a much larger scale: their agents could successfully complete tasks that they had previously failed at. We hadn't built a documentation tool. We'd built the context layer. The problem AI agents fail on real codebases without context. Ask an agent to work in a 20M line repo. It iterates with grep, ls, repeated searches. It takes forever, is incomplete, and is wildly non deterministic. That translates to frequent hallucinations, low trust, and outright task failure. Teams try to fix this with bigger context windows (doesn't scale), RAG methods (non exhaustive, semantic limitations, poor scaling), or manual context gathering (burns hours per engineer per week, inconsistent and unmaintainable at scale). None of them work. We took a different approach: pre compute exhaustive understanding ahead of time, make this fully automated and always up to date, then serve it to agents through MCP. \ "The better the documentation, the better the agent. Your context is better than anything else on the market."\ — VP Engineering, Leading Financial Software Provider How it works At the core is our Transpiler: an exhaustive compiler inspired system combining static analysis with LLM generation. It produces: 1. Symbol complete documentation for every file 2. Architecture overviews to guide agents and plan 3. Dependency maps for reviews 4. Commit complete changelogs We also build runtime sub agents optimized to shape pre computed context to the perfect package for a runtime task: Joint optimization between compile time and runtime. Every file visited, every symbol documented, relationships preserved. All kept current automatically as code changes. We expose this compiled output through carefully designed interface primitives callable via MCP or API. "I refactored the entire 1M line codebase with Driver and Cursor and I have no idea what it does. And with Driver, have no need to. The first thing I do is ask Cursor to research with Driver's MCP." — Head of Data Engineering, Global Trading Firm Traction We've deployed across 25+ enterprise customers , including high frequency trading firms and Fortune 500 companies, processing over 200 million lines of code in the last six months. Security SOC 2 Type II certified. Multi tenant SaaS, single tenant VPC, self hosted options, and GovCloud available. How to get started 1. Connect your SCM: Link Driver to GitHub, GitLab, Bitbucket or any other SCM system to ingest codebases and create context. 2. IdP & SSO: Set up SSO, enable SCIM, and map IdP groups to Driver teams to provide access to context for your organization. 3. Integrate Context Anywhere: User authenticated access via MCP server for interactive agents, M2M authentication for headless agents, and REST API for server side access. Our Ask Try it on your codebase at driver.ai (http://driver.ai) Large codebase and using Cursor/Claude Code? We'll set up MCP tools and you'll see the difference in a day. Enterprise with dozens of internal systems? We process all of them. Your agents get cross repo context. Onboarding engineers to legacy code? Architecture knowledge stops living in one person's head. "Within five hours, I built a functioning prototype that touched almost every area of our back end. It was supposed to take six weeks." — Director of Product, Leading Payments Platform

records.description: Kinro helps companies sell insurance through AI assistants such as ChatGPT. Selling insurance requires strict regulatory compliance, deep carrier integrations, and extremely reliable answers. Platforms like OpenAI and Google remain general-purpose and are less likely to build the vertical infrastructure required for regulated industries. Today, Kinro serves brokers and direct-to-consumer carriers across the US and Europe. Insurance distribution is huge: insurers pay roughly $100B+ in commissions annually, and distributors spend $10B+ every year on advertising to acquire customers. Pierre-Alexandre led research improving Gemini for financial services at DeepMind. Parth led infrastructure for training and inference at Zoox. Corentin was the first employee in an insurance startup and worked closely with many insurance leaders.
records.launchTitle: Kinro AI - AI sales agents for the insurance industry.
records.tagline: Enable brokers and D2C carriers to sell P&C insurance through AI.
records.launchBodyText: AI sales agents for the P&C insurance industry. Hi everyone! We are Pierre Alexandre Kamienny (https://www.linkedin.com/in/pakamienny/), Parth Ainampudi (https://www.linkedin.com/in/partha42/) and Corentin Hugot (https://www.linkedin.com/in/corentin hugot/), and we are building AI sales agents for insurance brokers and D2C carriers. The problem P&C insurance is a $1T industry where billions are spent every year on customer acquisition. Yet the buyer experience remains terrible, countless forms, going on call with licensed professionals… LLM interfaces (ChatGPT, Gemini, …) are taking over the buyer journey, but the experience remains an enhanced web search, sending the user to the normal flow again at the end. For insurance distributors, using AI in a customer facing environment means risking their license (giving recommendations, hallucinated policy details, …). Today, insurance is too often sold through heavy marketing and abusive sales practices, resulting in frustrated customers, poor understanding of coverage, and unreasonably high prices. @julianomass What we build Kinro (https://bookface.ycombinator.com/company/31382) builds AI sales agents for brokers and D2C carriers, automating the full sales flow from qualification and quoting to recommendations and binding. Here is our launch video: <https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=z bC6nejbbg Our agents can: Qualify buyers naturally Answer questions using T&Cs and the company's tone of voice Generate quotes in real time Compare options side by side Bind or hand off to a licensed agent We back every deployment with research grade evaluations run across thousands of simulated scenarios and production data, ensuring agents stay compliant with local regulations while continuously improving on sales performance and customer satisfaction. How we got here Parth spent years building RL systems for safety critical autonomous vehicles at Amazon. I led evaluation research for Gemini in financial services at Google DeepMind. Corentin spent years in InsurTech, working directly with brokers and carriers. We are all convinced consumers will find solutions to their financial problems in chat interfaces (OpenClaw, ChatGPT, and what comes next). Some of those solutions involve buying financial products. We want to enable these transactions. Our way of doing that is to enable sellers to be represented in these new generative interfaces compliantly, measurably, and at scale. Long term vision Today, agents interact with buyers directly in our clients' interfaces. Tomorrow, they will sell via agent to agent interactions on AI platforms (ChatGPT, Gemini, OpenClaw…). Every insurance product is distributed through a compliant intelligent agent across every surface. Super happy to be part of this community! Our ask We're closing deals with CMOs, Heads of Distribution, and AI leads at insurance comparators, brokers, and carriers that sell directly to consumers digitally. If you know anyone in that world, please connect us. We're hiring founding engineers. FDE, backend, ML research: pirates who want to define the standards of agent to agent sales. Super happy to be part of this community with you all. Pierre, Parth & Corentin kinro.ai (http://kinro.ai)
